INSTALL_LINE="install can /bin/false"
BLACKLIST_LINE="blacklist can"

mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d

if [[ ! -f "$CONF_FILE" ]]; then
    touch "$CONF_FILE"
fi

chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"
chown root:root "$CONF_FILE"

if grep -Eq '^\s*install\s+can\s+' "$CONF_FILE"; then
    if ! grep -Eq "^\s*install\s+can\s+/bin/false(\s|$)" "$CONF_FILE"; then
        sed -ri "s|^\s*install\s+can\s+.*|${INSTALL_LINE}|" "$CONF_FILE"
    fi
else
    echo "$INSTALL_LINE" >> "$CONF_FILE"
fi

if ! grep -Eq '^\s*blacklist\s+can(\s|$)' "$CONF_FILE"; then
    echo "$BLACKLIST_LINE" >> "$CONF_FILE"
fi

if lsmod | grep -Eq '^can\s'; then
    modprobe -r can 2>/dev/null || rmmod can 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if lsmod | grep -Eq '^can\s'; then

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure auditd records all executions of the privileged
# postdrop command by non-system users (auid >= 1000), tagged as a
# privileged command event. Idempotent and persistent across reboots.

set -u

PATTERN_BIN="/usr/sbin/postdrop"
KEY="privileged"
RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
DEFAULT_RULES_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/privileged.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

FULL_RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${PATTERN_BIN} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"

# Regex matching an existing rule for this binary (any key/auid variant styles)
MATCH_REGEX="^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+always,exit[[:space:]]+.*-F[[:space:]]+path=${PATTERN_BIN}([[:space:]]|$)"

ensure_rule_in_file() {
    local target_file="$1"
    local dir
    dir="$(dirname "${target_file}")"

    [ -d "${dir}" ] || mkdir -p "${dir}"

    # Search all .rules files in rules.d (or just the single file for audit.rules)
    local files=()
    if [ "${dir}" = "${RULES_D_DIR}" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            files+=("$f")
        done < <(find "${RULES_D_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.rules' -print0 2>/dev/null)
    else
        [ -f "${target_file}" ] && files+=("${target_file}")
    fi

    local found_file=""
    local f
    for f in "${files[@]}"; do
        if grep -Eq "${MATCH_REGEX}" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            found_file="$f"
            break
        fi
    done

    if [ -n "${found_file}" ]; then
        # Rule for this binary exists; normalize it to the required form if needed
        if ! grep -Fxq "${FULL_RULE}" "${found_file}"; then
            # Replace any matching line(s) with the canonical rule
            local tmp
            tmp="$(mktemp)"
            local replaced=0
            while IFS= read -r line; do
                if printf '%s\n' "${line}" | grep -Eq "${MATCH_REGEX}"; then
                    if [ "${replaced}" -eq 0 ]; then
                        printf '%s\n' "${FULL_RULE}" >> "${tmp}"
                        replaced=1
                    fi
                else
                    printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${tmp}"
                fi
            done < "${found_file}"
            cat "${tmp}" > "${found_file}"
            rm -f "${tmp}"
        fi
    else
        # No rule exists; append to the target file
        touch "${target_file}"
        # Ensure file ends with newline before appending
        if [ -s "${target_file}" ] && [ "$(tail -c1 "${target_file}" | wc -l)" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "" >> "${target_file}"
        fi
        printf '%s\n' "${FULL_RULE}" >> "${target_file}"
        chmod 0640 "${target_file}" 2>/dev/null || true
        chown root:root "${target_file}" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
}

# Persist rule in /etc/audit/rules.d/ (used by augenrules)
ensure_rule_in_file "${DEFAULT_RULES_FILE}"

# Also persist in /etc/audit/audit.rules if auditctl is used directly
# Determine which loader is active
USES_AUGENRULES=1
if [ -f /usr/lib/systemd/system/auditd.service ]; then
    if grep -Eq '^\s*ExecStartPost=.*auditctl' /usr/lib/systemd/system/auditd.service && \
       ! grep -Eq '^\s*ExecStartPost=.*augenrules' /usr/lib/systemd/system/auditd.service; then
        USES_AUGENRULES=0
    fi
fi

if [ "${USES_AUGENRULES}" -eq 0 ]; then
    ensure_rule_in_file "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Load rules into the running kernel (skip in immutable mode; takes effect on reboot)
IMMUTABLE=0
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq '^enabled[[:space:]]+2'; then
        IMMUTABLE=1
    fi
fi

if [ "${IMMUTABLE}" -eq 0 ]; then
    if [ "${USES_AUGENRULES}" -eq 1 ] && command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # Load only if not already active in kernel
        if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -Fq "path=${PATTERN_BIN}"; then
            auditctl -a always,exit -F "path=${PATTERN_BIN}" -F perm=x \
                -F 'auid>=1000' -F 'auid!=unset' -F "key=${KEY}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        fi
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure rsyslog authenticates the remote logging server
# using x509 certificate name verification before off-loading logs.
# (Enforces: $ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name)
#
set -u

MAIN_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
CONF_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
PARAM='$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode'
VALUE='x509/name'
CHANGED=0

# Must run as root
if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure rsyslog is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum -y install rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
        echo "ERROR: rsyslog package could not be installed." >&2
        exit 1
    }
    CHANGED=1
fi

# Build list of rsyslog configuration files
CONF_FILES=()
[ -f "$MAIN_CONF" ] && CONF_FILES+=("$MAIN_CONF")
if [ -d "$CONF_DIR" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r f; do
        CONF_FILES+=("$f")
    done < <(find "$CONF_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Ensure main conf exists
if [ ! -f "$MAIN_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$MAIN_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$MAIN_CONF"
    CONF_FILES+=("$MAIN_CONF")
fi

FOUND_CORRECT=0

# Fix any incorrect (active) occurrences of the directive in all conf files
for f in "${CONF_FILES[@]}"; do
    if grep -Eqi '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\b' "$f"; then
        # Correct any lines that do not specify x509/name
        if grep -Eqi '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+' "$f" && \
           ! grep -Eqi '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+x509/name\s*$' "$f"; then
            sed -ri 's|^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+.*$|'"$PARAM $VALUE"'|Ig' "$f"
            CHANGED=1
        fi
        if grep -Eqi '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+x509/name\s*$' "$f"; then
            FOUND_CORRECT=1
        fi
    fi
    # Also normalize RainerScript style StreamDriverAuthMode settings if present
    if grep -Eqi 'StreamDriverAuthMode\s*=\s*"' "$f"; then
        if grep -Eqi 'StreamDriverAuthMode\s*=\s*"[^"]*"' "$f" && \
           ! grep -Eqi 'StreamDriverAuthMode\s*=\s*"x509/name"' "$f"; then
            sed -ri 's|(StreamDriverAuthMode\s*=\s*")[^"]*(")|\1x509/name\2|Ig' "$f"
            CHANGED=1
        fi
        if grep -Eqi 'StreamDriverAuthMode\s*=\s*"x509/name"' "$f"; then
            FOUND_CORRECT=1
        fi
    fi
done

# If no correct active directive exists anywhere, append it to the main conf
if [ "$FOUND_CORRECT" -eq 0 ]; then
    {
        echo ""
        echo "# Added by STIG remediation: authenticate remote log server via x509 certificate name"
        echo "$PARAM $VALUE"
    } >> "$MAIN_CONF"
    CHANGED=1
fi

# Persist across reboots: ensure the service is enabled
if ! systemctl is-enabled rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1
    CHANGED=1
fi

# Restart rsyslog only if a change was made (idempotent behavior)
if [ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]; then
    systemctl restart rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
        echo "WARNING: rsyslog restart failed; verify configuration syntax." >&2
        exit 1
    }
    echo "Remediation applied: $PARAM set to $VALUE."
else
    echo "Already compliant: $PARAM is set to $VALUE."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

aide_conf="/etc/aide.conf"

if ! rpm -q aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum -y install aide
fi

if [[ ! -f "$aide_conf" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: $aide_conf not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

cp -p "$aide_conf" "${aide_conf}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

groups=$(LC_ALL=C grep -E '^[A-Z][A-Za-z_]*[[:space:]]*=' "$aide_conf" \
         | grep -v '^ALLXTRAHASHES' \
         | cut -f1 -d '=' | tr -d ' ' | sort -u)

changed=0

for group in $groups; do
    config=$(grep -E "^${group}[[:space:]]*=" "$aide_conf" | head -n1 \
             | cut -f2 -d '=' | tr -d ' ')

    if [[ "$config" != *acl* ]]; then
        if [[ -z "$config" ]]; then
            config="acl"
        else
            config="${config}+acl"
        fi
        sed -i "s/^${group}[[:space:]]*=.*/${group} = ${config}/g" "$aide_conf"
        changed=1
        echo "Updated ruleset '${group}' to include acl: ${config}"
    fi
done

if [[ "$changed" -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "All AIDE rulesets already include ACL verification. No changes made."
else
    echo "AIDE configuration updated. Consider re-initializing the AIDE database:"
    echo "  /usr/sbin/aide --init && mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz"
fi

exit 0

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

##############################################################################
# 1. systemd-coredump: do not write core dumps to disk (Storage=none,
#    ProcessSizeMax=0) in /etc/systemd/coredump.conf
##############################################################################
COREDUMP_CONF="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf"

if [ ! -f "$COREDUMP_CONF" ]; then
    printf '[Coredump]\n' > "$COREDUMP_CONF"
fi

if ! grep -qE '^\s*\[Coredump\]' "$COREDUMP_CONF"; then
    printf '\n[Coredump]\n' >> "$COREDUMP_CONF"
fi

set_coredump_opt() {
    local key="$1" value="$2"
    if grep -qE "^\s*${key}\s*=" "$COREDUMP_CONF"; then
        sed -ri "s|^\s*${key}\s*=.*|${key}=${value}|" "$COREDUMP_CONF"
    elif grep -qE "^\s*#\s*${key}\s*=" "$COREDUMP_CONF"; then
        sed -ri "0,/^\s*#\s*${key}\s*=.*/s||${key}=${value}|" "$COREDUMP_CONF"
    else
        sed -ri "s|^\s*\[Coredump\]\s*$|[Coredump]\n${key}=${value}|" "$COREDUMP_CONF"
    fi
}

set_coredump_opt "Storage" "none"
set_coredump_opt "ProcessSizeMax" "0"

chmod 0644 "$COREDUMP_CONF"

##############################################################################
# 2. PAM limits: hard core dump size limit of 0 for all users
##############################################################################
LIMITS_CONF="/etc/security/limits.conf"
LIMITS_DROPIN="/etc/security/limits.d/99-disable-coredumps.conf"

mkdir -p /etc/security/limits.d

# Remove any conflicting non-zero hard core entries in limits.conf
if grep -qE '^\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+' "$LIMITS_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    sed -ri 's|^\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+.*|* hard core 0|' "$LIMITS_CONF"
fi

# Authoritative drop-in (read last)
if [ ! -f "$LIMITS_DROPIN" ] || ! grep -qE '^\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+0\s*$' "$LIMITS_DROPIN"; then
    printf '* hard core 0\n' > "$LIMITS_DROPIN"
fi
chmod 0644 "$LIMITS_DROPIN"

##############################################################################
# 3. systemd default limit: DefaultLimitCORE=0 for services
##############################################################################
SYSTEM_CONF="/etc/systemd/system.conf"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

##############################################################################
# 1. systemd-coredump: do not write core dumps to disk (Storage=none,
#    ProcessSizeMax=0) in /etc/systemd/coredump.conf
##############################################################################
COREDUMP_CONF="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf"

if [ ! -f "$COREDUMP_CONF" ]; then
    printf '[Coredump]\n' > "$COREDUMP_CONF"
fi

if ! grep -qE '^\s*\[Coredump\]' "$COREDUMP_CONF"; then
    printf '\n[Coredump]\n' >> "$COREDUMP_CONF"
fi

set_coredump_opt() {
    local key="$1" value="$2"
    if grep -qE "^\s*${key}\s*=" "$COREDUMP_CONF"; then
        sed -ri "s|^\s*${key}\s*=.*|${key}=${value}|" "$COREDUMP_CONF"
    elif grep -qE "^\s*#\s*${key}\s*=" "$COREDUMP_CONF"; then
        sed -ri "0,/^\s*#\s*${key}\s*=.*/s||${key}=${value}|" "$COREDUMP_CONF"
    else
        sed -ri "s|^\s*\[Coredump\]\s*$|[Coredump]\n${key}=${value}|" "$COREDUMP_CONF"
    fi
}

set_coredump_opt "Storage" "none"
set_coredump_opt "ProcessSizeMax" "0"

chmod 0644 "$COREDUMP_CONF"

##############################################################################
# 2. PAM limits: hard core dump size limit of 0 for all users
##############################################################################
LIMITS_CONF="/etc/security/limits.conf"
LIMITS_DROPIN="/etc/security/limits.d/99-disable-coredumps.conf"

mkdir -p /etc/security/limits.d

# Remove any conflicting non-zero hard core entries in limits.conf
if grep -qE '^\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+' "$LIMITS_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    sed -ri 's|^\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+.*|* hard core 0|' "$LIMITS_CONF"
fi

# Authoritative drop-in (read last)
if [ ! -f "$LIMITS_DROPIN" ] || ! grep -qE '^\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+0\s*$' "$LIMITS_DROPIN"; then
    printf '* hard core 0\n' > "$LIMITS_DROPIN"
fi
chmod 0644 "$LIMITS_DROPIN"

##############################################################################
# 3. systemd default limit: DefaultLimitCORE=0 for services
##############################################################################
SYSTEM_CONF="/etc/systemd/system.conf"

if grep -qE '^\s*DefaultLimitCORE\s*=' "$SYSTEM_CONF"; then
    sed -ri 's|^\s*DefaultLimitCORE\s*=.*|DefaultLimitCORE=0|' "$SYSTEM_CONF"
elif grep -qE '^\s*#\s*DefaultLimitCORE\s*=' "$SYSTEM_CONF"; then
    sed -ri '0,/^\s*#\s*DefaultLimitCORE\s*=.*/s||DefaultLimitCORE=0|' "$SYSTEM_CONF"
else
    printf 'DefaultLimitCORE=0\n' >> "$SYSTEM_CONF"
fi

# 4. Kernel: prevent core dumps of setuid programs (persistent + runtime)
##############################################################################

    printf 'fs.suid_dumpable = 0\n' > "$SYSCTL_DROPIN"
fi
chmod 0644 "$SYSCTL_DROPIN"

# Remove conflicting settings from other sysctl config files

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Audit all executions of /usr/sbin/semanage (privileged command)
# Applies to RHEL 8. Idempotent and persistent across reboots/auditd restarts.
#

set -euo pipefail

# Must run as root
if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/privileged.rules"
AUDIT_KEY="privileged"
SEMANAGE_PATH="/usr/sbin/semanage"

# Rule capturing regular users (auid >= 1000, excluding unset/-1)
RULE_USERS="-a always,exit -F path=${SEMANAGE_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${AUDIT_KEY}"
# Rule capturing root (auid = 0)
RULE_ROOT="-a always,exit -F path=${SEMANAGE_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid=0 -F key=${AUDIT_KEY}"

# Ensure the audit package/daemon is present
if ! command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf -y install audit >/dev/null
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum -y install audit >/dev/null
    else
        echo "ERROR: audit package not installed and no package manager found." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Ensure rules directory and file exist
mkdir -p "${RULES_DIR}"
touch "${RULES_FILE}"
chmod 0640 "${RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${RULES_FILE}"

changed=0

# Helper: add a rule line if not already present (matching on any equivalent form)
add_rule_if_missing() {
    local rule="$1"
    # Normalize check: look for an existing non-comment line in rules.d that audits
    # semanage execution with the same auid filter.
    local auid_filter
    auid_filter="$(echo "${rule}" | grep -oE 'auid(>=|=|!=)[^ ]+' | head -n1)"
    if ! grep -rhsE "^[^#]*-a[[:space:]]+always,exit" "${RULES_DIR}" 2>/dev/null \
        | grep -F -- "path=${SEMANAGE_PATH}" \
        | grep -F -- "perm=x" \
        | grep -qF -- "${auid_filter}"; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
        changed=1
    fi
}

add_rule_if_missing "${RULE_USERS}"
add_rule_if_missing "${RULE_ROOT}"

# Enable and start auditd for persistence across reboots
systemctl enable auditd.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
if ! systemctl is-active --quiet auditd.service; then
    # auditd refuses systemctl start in some configs; fall back to service
    systemctl start auditd.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || service auditd start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# Load the merged rules into the running kernel
if [ "${changed}" -eq 1 ] || ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qF -- "path=${SEMANAGE_PATH}"; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "WARNING: could not load audit rules into the running kernel." >&2
        fi
    else
        auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

# Check for immutable audit configuration (-e 2): rules load only at boot
if auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '^enabled[[:space:]]+2'; then
    echo "NOTICE: Audit system is in immutable mode; a reboot is required for the rule to take effect."
fi

# Verify persistence configuration
if grep -qF -- "path=${SEMANAGE_PATH}" "${RULES_FILE}"; then
    echo "OK: semanage audit rules are configured in ${RULES_FILE} (key=${AUDIT_KEY})."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to configure semanage audit rules." >&2
    exit 1
fi

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
PARAM="RekeyLimit"
VALUE="512M 1h"

if [ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $SSHD_CONFIG not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    sed -ri "s/^\s*${PARAM}\s+.*$/${PARAM} ${VALUE}/" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    if grep -Eq "^\s*#\s*${PARAM}\b" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        sed -ri "0,/^\s*#\s*${PARAM}\b.*$/s//${PARAM} ${VALUE}/" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    else
        printf '\n%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

count=$(grep -Ec "^\s*${PARAM}\s+" "$SSHD_CONFIG" || true)
if [ "$count" -gt 1 ]; then
    awk -v param="$PARAM" -v value="$VALUE" '
        $0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*"param"[[:space:]]+" {
            if (!seen) { print param" "value; seen=1 }
            next
        }
        { print }
    ' "$SSHD_CONFIG" > "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    cat "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp" > "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    rm -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
fi

chown root:root "$SSHD_CONFIG"
chmod 0600 "$SSHD_CONFIG"

if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t; then
        echo "ERROR: sshd configuration validation failed." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
    systemctl restart sshd
fi

exit 0

    local rules_file="$1"

    local rules_dir
    rules_dir="$(dirname "$rules_file")"
    [ -d "$rules_dir" ] || mkdir -p "$rules_dir"

    if [ -f "$rules_file" ]; then
        # Find lines matching an existing rule for this path
        local matched_line
        matched_line="$(grep -E "${ACTION_ARCH_REGEX}.*${PATH_REGEX}" "$rules_file" 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)"

        if [ -n "$matched_line" ]; then

                local escaped_rule

                sed -i -E "/${ACTION_ARCH_REGEX}.*${PATH_REGEX}/d" "$rules_file"
                echo "$FULL_RULE" >> "$rules_file"
            fi
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    touch "$rules_file"
    chmod 0640 "$rules_file"

        if grep -Eq "${ACTION_ARCH_REGEX}.*${PATH_REGEX}" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then

        fi
    else

        if grep -Pqi "^\s*#\s*${key}\b" "$file"; then
            sed -ri "0,/^\s*#\s*${key}\b.*$/s//${key} ${MIN_ROUNDS}/I" "$file"

            echo "${key} ${MIN_ROUNDS}" >> "$file"

        rm -f "${file}.tmp"
    fi
}

ensure_rounds "SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS" "$LOGIN_DEFS"
ensure_rounds "SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS" "$LOGIN_DEFS"

# Sanity: MAX must not be lower than MIN (both are now >= 5000, but keep consistent)

max_val="$(grep -Pi '^\s*SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS\s+\S+' "$LOGIN_DEFS" | tail -n 1 | awk '{print $2}')"
if [ -n "$min_val" ] && [ -n "$max_val" ] && [ "$max_val" -lt "$min_val" ]; then
    sed -ri "s/^\s*(SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS)\s+\S+.*$/\1 ${min_val}/Ig" "$LOGIN_DEFS"

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -u

MOUNT_POINT="/boot"
REQUIRED_OPT="nosuid"
FSTAB="/etc/fstab"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if ! findmnt --kernel "${MOUNT_POINT}" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "INFO: ${MOUNT_POINT} is not a separately mounted filesystem. No remediation required."
    exit 0
fi

fstab_entry="$(findmnt --fstab --noheadings --target "${MOUNT_POINT}" --output TARGET 2>/dev/null | head -n1)"

if [ "${fstab_entry}" != "${MOUNT_POINT}" ]; then
    echo "WARNING: No explicit ${FSTAB} entry found for ${MOUNT_POINT}. Attempting to add one from current mount info."
    src="$(findmnt --kernel --noheadings --output SOURCE "${MOUNT_POINT}")"
    fstype="$(findmnt --kernel --noheadings --output FSTYPE "${MOUNT_POINT}")"
    if [ -n "${src}" ] && [ -n "${fstype}" ]; then
        uuid="$(blkid -s UUID -o value "${src}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
        if [ -n "${uuid}" ]; then
            src="UUID=${uuid}"
        fi
        cp -p "${FSTAB}" "${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
        printf '%s %s %s defaults,%s 0 0\n' "${src}" "${MOUNT_POINT}" "${fstype}" "${REQUIRED_OPT}" >> "${FSTAB}"
        echo "INFO: Added ${FSTAB} entry for ${MOUNT_POINT} with ${REQUIRED_OPT}."
    else
        echo "ERROR: Unable to determine source/fstype for ${MOUNT_POINT}; cannot persist setting." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
else
    current_opts="$(findmnt --fstab --noheadings --target "${MOUNT_POINT}" --output OPTIONS | head -n1)"
    if printf '%s' ",${current_opts}," | grep -q ",${REQUIRED_OPT},"; then
        echo "INFO: ${FSTAB} entry for ${MOUNT_POINT} already contains ${REQUIRED_OPT}."
    else
        cp -p "${FSTAB}" "${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
        awk -v mp="${MOUNT_POINT}" -v opt="${REQUIRED_OPT}" '
        {
            if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ || NF < 4) { print; next }
            if ($2 == mp) {
                n = split($4, opts, ",")
                found = 0
                for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
                    if (opts[i] == opt) { found = 1; break }
                }
                if (!found) {
                    $4 = $4 "," opt
                }
                out = $1 " " $2 " " $3 " " $4
                for (i = 5; i <= NF; i++) { out = out " " $i }
                print out
                next
            }
            print
        }' "${FSTAB}" > "${FSTAB}.tmp.$$" && mv "${FSTAB}.tmp.$$" "${FSTAB}"
        echo "INFO: Added ${REQUIRED_OPT} to ${FSTAB} entry for ${MOUNT_POINT}."
    fi
fi

runtime_opts="$(findmnt --kernel --noheadings --output OPTIONS "${MOUNT_POINT}")"
if printf '%s' ",${runtime_opts}," | grep -q ",${REQUIRED_OPT},"; then
    echo "INFO: ${MOUNT_POINT} is already mounted with ${REQUIRED_OPT} on the running system."
else
    if mount -o "remount,${REQUIRED_OPT}" "${MOUNT_POINT}"; then
        echo "INFO: Remounted ${MOUNT_POINT} with ${REQUIRED_OPT}."
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to remount ${MOUNT_POINT} with ${REQUIRED_OPT}." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

runtime_opts="$(findmnt --kernel --noheadings --output OPTIONS "${MOUNT_POINT}")"
if printf '%s' ",${runtime_opts}," | grep -q ",${REQUIRED_OPT},"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${MOUNT_POINT} is mounted with ${REQUIRED_OPT} and the setting is persisted in ${FSTAB}."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: ${MOUNT_POINT} is still not mounted with ${REQUIRED_OPT}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

        }' "${FSTAB}" > "${FSTAB}.tmp.$$" && mv "${FSTAB}.tmp.$$" "${FSTAB}"
        echo "INFO: Added ${REQUIRED_OPT} to ${FSTAB} entry for ${MOUNT_POINT}."
    fi
fi

runtime_opts="$(findmnt --kernel --noheadings --output OPTIONS "${MOUNT_POINT}")"
if printf '%s' ",${runtime_opts}," | grep -q ",${REQUIRED_OPT},"; then
    echo "INFO: ${MOUNT_POINT} is already mounted with ${REQUIRED_OPT} on the running system."
else
    if mount -o "remount,${REQUIRED_OPT}" "${MOUNT_POINT}"; then
        echo "INFO: Remounted ${MOUNT_POINT} with ${REQUIRED_OPT}."
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to remount ${MOUNT_POINT} with ${REQUIRED_OPT}." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

runtime_opts="$(findmnt --kernel --noheadings --output OPTIONS "${MOUNT_POINT}")"
if printf '%s' ",${runtime_opts}," | grep -q ",${REQUIRED_OPT},"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${MOUNT_POINT} is mounted with ${REQUIRED_OPT} and the setting is persisted in ${FSTAB}."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: ${MOUNT_POINT} is still not mounted with ${REQUIRED_OPT}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#
# Remediation: Record events that modify file permissions via chmod
# Rule: audit_rules_dac_modification_chmod (RHEL 8)
#
# Adds audit rules for the chmod syscall for both 32-bit and 64-bit
# architectures, restricted to real users (auid>=1000, auid!=unset),
# tagged with the key "perm_mod". Idempotent and persistent.

set -eu

KEY="perm_mod"
SYSCALL="chmod"
AUID_MIN="1000"
RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/audit_rules_dac_modification_${SYSCALL}.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

mkdir -p "${RULES_D_DIR}"
touch "${RULES_FILE}"
chmod 0640 "${RULES_FILE}"

# Regex fragment that matches an existing equivalent rule for a given arch
rule_exists() {
    local arch="$1"
    local file_or_dir="$2"
    grep -rPq -- \
"^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+always,exit[[:space:]]+-F[[:space:]]+arch=${arch}\b.*(-S[[:space:]]+(\S+,)*${SYSCALL}(,\S+)*)\b.*-F[[:space:]]+auid>=${AUID_MIN}\b.*-F[[:space:]]+auid!=(unset|-1|4294967295)\b.*(-k[[:space:]]+\S+|-F[[:space:]]+key=\S+)" \
        "${file_or_dir}" 2>/dev/null
}

for ARCH in b32 b64; do
    RULE="-a always,exit -F arch=${ARCH} -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid>=${AUID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"

    # Persistent rules for augenrules (loaded automatically when auditd starts)
    if ! rule_exists "${ARCH}" "${RULES_D_DIR}"; then
        echo "${RULE}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    fi

    # Also ensure legacy /etc/audit/audit.rules covers auditctl load mode
    if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
        if ! rule_exists "${ARCH}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
            echo "${RULE}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
        fi
    fi
done

# Reload rules into the running kernel if auditd is active
if pgrep -x auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#
# Remediation: Enforce a minimum password length of 15 characters (RHEL 8)
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times; changes persist across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

MIN_LEN=15

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Configure pam_pwquality (governs password quality for new passwords)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"

# Ensure the file exists (it ships with libpwquality on RHEL 8)
if [[ ! -f "$PWQUALITY_CONF" ]]; then
    touch "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
fi

if grep -Eq '^\s*minlen\s*=' "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
    # Replace any existing active minlen setting with the required value
    sed -ri "s/^\s*minlen\s*=.*/minlen = ${MIN_LEN}/" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
else
    # Append the setting (also handles the case where it is only commented out)
    printf '\nminlen = %s\n' "$MIN_LEN" >> "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
fi

# Fix any drop-in overrides that might weaken the policy
if [[ -d /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d ]]; then
    for f in /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf; do
        [[ -e "$f" ]] || continue
        if grep -Eq '^\s*minlen\s*=' "$f"; then
            sed -ri "s/^\s*minlen\s*=.*/minlen = ${MIN_LEN}/" "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Configure /etc/login.defs (system-wide login/account policy)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"

if [[ ! -f "$LOGIN_DEFS" ]]; then
    touch "$LOGIN_DEFS"
    chmod 0644 "$LOGIN_DEFS"
fi

if grep -Eq '^\s*PASS_MIN_LEN\b' "$LOGIN_DEFS"; then
    sed -ri "s/^\s*PASS_MIN_LEN\s+.*/PASS_MIN_LEN ${MIN_LEN}/" "$LOGIN_DEFS"
else
    printf '\nPASS_MIN_LEN %s\n' "$MIN_LEN" >> "$LOGIN_DEFS"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Verify
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
grep -Eq "^\s*minlen\s*=\s*${MIN_LEN}\b" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" || {
    echo "ERROR: failed to set minlen in $PWQUALITY_CONF" >&2
    exit 1
}
grep -Eq "^\s*PASS_MIN_LEN\s+${MIN_LEN}\b" "$LOGIN_DEFS" || {
    echo "ERROR: failed to set PASS_MIN_LEN in $LOGIN_DEFS" >&2
    exit 1
}

echo "Remediation complete: minimum password length set to ${MIN_LEN} characters."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

FAILLOCK_DIR="/var/log/faillock"
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
PAM_FILES="/etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth"

#
# 1. Create the persistent faillock directory with correct ownership,
#    permissions, and SELinux context so records survive a reboot.
#
mkdir -p "$FAILLOCK_DIR"
chown root:root "$FAILLOCK_DIR"
chmod 0755 "$FAILLOCK_DIR"

if command -v semanage >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! semanage fcontext -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${FAILLOCK_DIR}(/.\*)?"; then
        semanage fcontext -a -t faillog_t "${FAILLOCK_DIR}(/.*)?" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
fi
if command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    restorecon -R -F "$FAILLOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

#
# 2. Point pam_faillock at the persistent directory via faillock.conf.
#
touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
chown root:root "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
chmod 0644 "$FAILLOCK_CONF"

    if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*dir[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${FAILLOCK_DIR}[[:space:]]*$" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*dir[[:space:]]*=.*$|dir = ${FAILLOCK_DIR}|" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
elif grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*dir[[:space:]]*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    # Uncomment and set the commented-out example line

else
    printf 'dir = %s\n' "$FAILLOCK_DIR" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

#
# 3. Ensure the authselect profile has faillock enabled so the PAM stack

#
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1 && authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then

    fi
    authselect apply-changes -b 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# 4. Fix any inline dir= arguments on pam_faillock lines in the PAM stack

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Enforce a finite sudo credential-cache (re-authentication) timeout on RHEL 8.
# Sets "Defaults timestamp_timeout=<N>" where N is a short, non-negative value.
# A negative value (never expire) is explicitly removed anywhere it is found.
# Idempotent: safe to run repeatedly.

set -euo pipefail

TIMEOUT=5   # minutes; must be >= 0 (never negative / never "never expire")
DROPIN="/etc/sudoers.d/00-remediate-timestamp_timeout"
DESIRED="Defaults timestamp_timeout=${TIMEOUT}"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if ! command -v visudo >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: visudo not found; is sudo installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

changed=0

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Scrub any existing timestamp_timeout settings from sudoers files
#    (except our managed drop-in), so no conflicting or infinite value wins.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
files=(/etc/sudoers)
if [[ -d /etc/sudoers.d ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        files+=("$f")
    done < <(find /etc/sudoers.d -maxdepth 1 -type f ! -name "$(basename "$DROPIN")" -print0)
fi

for f in "${files[@]}"; do
    [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*Defaults[^#]*\btimestamp_timeout\b' "$f"; then
        tmp="$(mktemp)"
        # Remove the timestamp_timeout option token from Defaults lines,
        # clean up leftover commas, and drop lines that become empty Defaults.
        sed -E \
            -e '/^[[:space:]]*Defaults[^#]*\btimestamp_timeout\b/ s/,?[[:space:]]*\btimestamp_timeout\b[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*-?[0-9]+//g' \
            -e 's/^([[:space:]]*Defaults[^,[:space:]]*)[[:space:]]*,[[:space:]]*/\1 /' \
            "$f" | sed -E '/^[[:space:]]*Defaults[[:alnum:]_@:>!]*[[:space:]]*$/d' > "$tmp"

        if ! cmp -s "$f" "$tmp"; then
            # Validate modified file before installing it
            if visudo -cf "$tmp" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                chmod --reference="$f" "$tmp" 2>/dev/null || chmod 0440 "$tmp"
                chown --reference="$f" "$tmp" 2>/dev/null || chown root:root "$tmp"
                cp -f "$tmp" "$f"
                changed=1
                echo "INFO: Removed conflicting timestamp_timeout entries from $f"
            else
                echo "ERROR: Refusing to install invalid sudoers edit for $f" >&2
                rm -f "$tmp"
                exit 1
            fi
        fi
        rm -f "$tmp"
    fi
done

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Enforce the desired finite timeout via a managed drop-in file.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
mkdir -p /etc/sudoers.d
chmod 0750 /etc/sudoers.d 2>/dev/null || true

current=""
[[ -f "$DROPIN" ]] && current="$(cat "$DROPIN")"

if [[ "$current" != "$DESIRED" ]]; then
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    printf '%s\n' "$DESIRED" > "$tmp"
    if visudo -cf "$tmp" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        cp -f "$tmp" "$DROPIN"
        changed=1
        echo "INFO: Enforced '${DESIRED}' in ${DROPIN}"
    else
        echo "ERROR: Generated drop-in failed visudo validation." >&2
        rm -f "$tmp"
        exit 1
    fi
    rm -f "$tmp"
fi

chown root:root "$DROPIN"
chmod 0440 "$DROPIN"

# ---

LOCK_FILE="${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}/00-security-smartcard"

CHANGED=0

mkdir -p "$DCONF_PROFILE_DIR" "$DCONF_DB_DIR" "$DCONF_LOCK_DIR"

# Ensure the dconf user profile references both the user db and the local system db
if [ ! -f "$DCONF_PROFILE" ]; then
    printf 'user-db:user\nsystem-db:local\n' > "$DCONF_PROFILE"
    CHANGED=1
else
    if ! grep -qx 'user-db:user' "$DCONF_PROFILE"; then
        sed -i '1i user-db:user' "$DCONF_PROFILE"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
    if ! grep -qx 'system-db:local' "$DCONF_PROFILE"; then
        printf 'system-db:local\n' >> "$DCONF_PROFILE"
        CHANGED=1
    fi

removal-action='lock-screen'
EOF

if [ ! -f "$SETTINGS_FILE" ] || [ "$(cat "$SETTINGS_FILE")" != "$SETTINGS_CONTENT" ]; then

/org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard/removal-action
EOF

if [ ! -f "$LOCK_FILE" ] || [ "$(cat "$LOCK_FILE")" != "$LOCK_CONTENT" ]; then

    CHANGED=1
fi

# Ensure sane permissions
chmod 0644 "$DCONF_PROFILE" "$SETTINGS_FILE" "$LOCK_FILE"
chown root:root "$DCONF_PROFILE" "$SETTINGS_FILE" "$LOCK_FILE"

# Rebuild the dconf databases if anything changed (or if db is missing)
if [ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ] || [ ! -f /etc/dconf/db/local ]; then
    dconf update
    echo "Remediation applied: smartcard removal will lock the screen (locked system-wide)."
else
    echo "System already compliant: smartcard removal lock-screen policy is in place."
fi

exit 0

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1

if ! command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "systemctl not found; cannot remediate." >&2
    exit 1
fi

        systemctl stop systemd-coredump.socket || rc=1

PARAM="PermitRootLogin"
VALUE="no"

    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found. Is openssh-server installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

changed=0

# Remove any conflicting settings from Include drop-in files (if present)
if grep -Eq '^\s*Include\s' "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null; then
    for dropin in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$dropin" ] || continue
        if grep -Eiq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+" "$dropin"; then
            if grep -Eiq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+(?!${VALUE}\b)" "$dropin" 2>/dev/null || \
               ! grep -Eiq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+${VALUE}\s*$" "$dropin"; then
                sed -ri "s/^\s*(${PARAM}\s+.*)$/#\1/I" "$dropin"
                changed=1
            fi
        fi
    done
fi

if grep -Eiq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    if ! grep -Eiq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+${VALUE}\s*$" "$SSHD_CONFIG" || \
       [ "$(grep -Eic "^\s*${PARAM}\s+" "$SSHD_CONFIG")" -gt 1 ]; then
        cp -p "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
        sed -ri "s/^\s*(${PARAM}\s+.*)$/#\1/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
        printf '%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
        changed=1
    fi
else

    printf '%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    changed=1
fi

# Verify effective runtime configuration
effective="$(sshd -T 2>/dev0/null | awk 'tolower($1)=="permitrootlogin" {print tolower($2)}')" 2>/dev/null
effective="$(sshd -T 2>/dev/null | awk 'tolower($1)=="permitrootlogin" {print tolower($2)}')"

if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    # Validate config syntax before restarting
    if sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
        systemctl restart sshd.service 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart ssh.service 2>/dev/null
        echo "Remediation applied: ${PARAM} set to ${VALUE}; sshd restarted."

        exit 1
    fi

fi

    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found. Is openssh-server installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

if grep -Eq '^\s*Include\s' "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null; then

        [ -e "$dropin" ] || continue
        if grep -Eiq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+" "$dropin"; then

               ! grep -Eiq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+${VALUE}\s*$" "$dropin"; then
                sed -ri "s/^\s*(${PARAM}\s+.*)$/#\1/I" "$dropin"

            fi
        fi
    done
fi

# Comment out any existing incorrect (active) PermitRootLogin lines in main config
if grep -Eiq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    if ! grep -Eiq "^\s*${PARAM}\s+${VALUE}\s*$" "$SSHD_CONFIG" || \
       [ "$(grep -Eic "^\s*${PARAM}\s+" "$SSHD_CONFIG")" -gt 1 ]; then
        cp -p "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
        sed -ri "s/^\s*(${PARAM}\s+.*)$/#\1/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
        printf '%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"

    fi

    printf '%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"

#
# Remediation: Audit all uses of the setxattr syscall on RHEL 8
# - Covers auid>=1000 (interactive users) and auid=0 (root)
# - Covers both b32 and b64 syscall interfaces
# - Tags records with the "perm_mod" key
# - Persistent and compatible with both augenrules and auditctl loaders
# - Idempotent: safe to run multiple times
#

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S setxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S setxattr -F auid=0 -k ${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S setxattr -F auid=0 -k ${KEY}"
)

#
# Remediation: Ensure auditd records all uses of the init_module syscall
# (kernel module loading) by users with auid >= 1000, on RHEL 8.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/module_load.rules"
KEY="module_load"
SYSCALL="init_module"

# Must be root to modify audit configuration
if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"

# Determine which architectures to cover:
#  - 32-bit rule always applies
#  - 64-bit rule only on a 64-bit kernel
ARCHS=("b32")
if [ "$(getconf LONG_BIT 2>/dev/null || echo 32)" = "64" ]; then
    ARCHS+=("b64")
fi

changed=0

for arch in "${ARCHS[@]}"; do
    rule="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"

    # Check all persistent rule files for an equivalent existing rule
    if grep -rhE \

        "$RULES_DIR"/*.rules 2>/dev/null | grep -qE "(-k[[:space:]]+|key=)"; then
        continue
    fi

    echo "$rule" >> "$RULES_FILE"
    changed=1
done

# Enforce sane ownership/permissions on the rules file if it exists
if [ -f "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
    chown root:root "$RULES_FILE"
    chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"
fi

# Load the merged rules into the running audit daemon so the rule takes
# effect immediately as well as persisting across reboots.
if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load || echo "WARNING: augenrules --load failed; rules will apply on next reboot." >&2
    else
        service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || \
            echo "WARNING: could not reload auditd; rules will apply on next reboot." >&2
    fi
fi

exit 0

    chown root:root "$RULES_FILE"
    chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"
fi

# Load the merged rules into the running audit daemon so the rule takes
# effect immediately as well as persisting across reboots.
if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load || echo "WARNING: augenrules --load failed; rules will apply on next reboot." >&2
    else
        service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || \
            echo "WARNING: could not reload auditd; rules will apply on next reboot." >&2
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Configure auditd to record unsuccessful file open attempts
# (EACCES and EPERM) for users with auid >= 1000 (excluding unset),
# on both b32 and b64 architectures, keyed with "access".
# Rules are persisted in /etc/audit/rules.d/ and loaded via augenrules.
#

set -eu

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/access.rules"

# Must be root
if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure audit package/tools are present
if ! command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: audit package (auditctl) not found. Install 'audit' first." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure rules directory exists
mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"

# Ensure rules file exists with secure permissions
if [ ! -f "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
    touch "$RULES_FILE"
fi
chown root:root "$RULES_FILE"
chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"

# Desired audit rules
RULES=(
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S creat,open,openat,open_by_handle_at,truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S creat,open,openat,open_by_handle_at,truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S creat,open,openat,open_by_handle_at,truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S creat,open,openat,open_by_handle_at,truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access"
)

CHANGED=0
for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -qxF -- "$rule" "$RULES_FILE"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$RULES_FILE"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done

    if grep -qs '^ExecStartPost=.*augenrules' /usr/lib/systemd/system/auditd.service 2>/dev/null \
       || command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load
    else
        auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules
    fi
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -u

AUDITD_CONF="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"
PARAM="disk_full_action"
VALUE="single"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$AUDITD_CONF" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $AUDITD_CONF not found. Is the audit package installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

current_value="$(grep -Ei "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDITD_CONF" | tail -n 1 | awk -F= '{gsub(/[[:space:]]/,"",$2); print tolower($2)}')"

if [ "$current_value" = "$VALUE" ]; then
    echo "OK: ${PARAM} is already set to '${VALUE}' in ${AUDITD_CONF}."
else
    cp -p "$AUDITD_CONF" "${AUDITD_CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

    if grep -Eqi "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDITD_CONF"; then
        sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=.*/${PARAM} = ${VALUE}/I" "$AUDITD_CONF"
    else
        printf '\n%s = %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$AUDITD_CONF"
    fi

    echo "CHANGED: Set ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} in ${AUDITD_CONF}."
fi

if ! grep -Eqi "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "$AUDITD_CONF"; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to verify ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} in ${AUDITD_CONF}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if systemctl is-enabled auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "OK: auditd service is enabled and configuration will persist across reboots."
else
    systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if systemctl is-active auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if service auditd restart >/dev/null 2>&1 || service auditd reload >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: auditd restarted; new ${PARAM} setting is active."
    else
        echo "WARNING: Could not restart auditd automatically; setting will take effect on next reboot or manual restart." >&2
    fi
else
    echo "NOTE: auditd is not currently running; setting will apply when auditd starts."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

GRUB_DEFAULT="/etc/default/grub"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#
# 1. Ensure audit=1 is present in GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX in /etc/default/grub
#    so that any future kernels inherit the setting.
#
touch "$GRUB_DEFAULT"

if grep -q '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_DEFAULT"; then
    current_line=$(grep '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_DEFAULT" | tail -n 1)

    if echo "$current_line" | grep -Eq '(^|[" ])audit=1([" ]|$)'; then
        : # already correct
    elif echo "$current_line" | grep -Eq '(^|[" ])audit=[^" ]*'; then
        # audit= exists with a wrong value; fix it in place
        sed -ri 's/(^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*)\baudit=[^" ]*/\1audit=1/' "$GRUB_DEFAULT"
    else
        # append audit=1 inside the existing quoted value
        sed -ri 's/^(\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=")(.*)"$/\1\2 audit=1"/' "$GRUB_DEFAULT"
        # handle unquoted or empty variants gracefully
        if ! grep '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_DEFAULT" | tail -n 1 | grep -Eq '(^|[" ])audit=1([" ]|$)'; then
            sed -ri 's/^(\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=)(.*)$/\1"\2 audit=1"/' "$GRUB_DEFAULT"
        fi
        # clean up possible leading space inside quotes
        sed -ri 's/^(\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=")\s+/\1/' "$GRUB_DEFAULT"
    fi
else
    echo 'GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="audit=1"' >> "$GRUB_DEFAULT"
fi

#
# 2. Apply audit=1 to all currently installed kernels via grubby.
#    Remove any existing audit= argument first to normalize, then add audit=1.
#
if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --remove-args="audit" 2>/dev/null || true
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="audit=1"
else
    echo "grubby not found; cannot update installed kernel entries." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#
# 3. Verify the end state.
#
fail=0
while read -r kernel_args; do
    if ! echo "$kernel_args" | grep -Eq '(^| )audit=1( |$)'; then
        fail=1
    fi
done < <(grubby --info=ALL | grep '^args=' | sed -e 's/^args=//' -e 's/^"//' -e 's/"$//')

if grep '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_DEFAULT" | tail -n 1 | grep -Eq '(^|[" ])audit=1([" ]|$)'; then
    :
else
    fail=1
fi

if [ "$fail" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Remediation complete: audit=1 is set for all installed kernels and in $GRUB_DEFAULT."
    exit 0
else
    echo "Remediation verification failed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# Ensure the audit package is installed
if ! rpm -q audit >/dev/null 2>&1; then

fi

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/modules.rules"

mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"
touch "$RULES_FILE"
chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"

CHANGED=0

# Function: ensure an audit rule exists for a given syscall/arch combo
ensure_rule() {
    local arch="$1"
    local syscall="$2"
    local rule="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${syscall} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k modules"

    # Search all persistent rule files for an equivalent existing rule:
    # must be an always,exit rule for the same arch and syscall, filtered on
    # auid>=1000, excluding unset auid, keyed with 'modules'.
    if grep -rhE -- "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)\b" "$RULES_DIR"/*.rules 2>/dev/null \
        | grep -E -- "arch=${arch}\b" \
        | grep -E -- "(-S[[:space:]]+[^[:space:]]*\b${syscall}\b|${syscall})" \
        | grep -E -- "auid>=1000" \
        | grep -E -- "auid!=(unset|-1|4294967295)" \
        | grep -qE -- "(-k[[:space:]]+modules\b|-F[[:space:]]+key=modules\b)"; then
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$rule" >> "$RULES_FILE"
    CHANGED=1
}

# Determine applicable architectures
ARCHS=("b32")
case "$(uname -m)" in
    x86_64|aarch64|ppc64|ppc64le|s390x)
        ARCHS+=("b64")
        ;;
esac

for arch in "${ARCHS[@]}"; do
    for sc in init_module finit_module; do
        ensure_rule "$arch" "$sc"
    done
done

# Ensure auditd is enabled and running so rules are enforced
systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
if ! systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    service auditd start >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl start auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if [ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    else
        service auditd restart >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi

    # If audit is in immutable mode (enabled=2), rules load only after reboot
    if auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -qE "^enabled[[:space:]]+2"; then
        echo "NOTE: Audit system is in immutable mode; a reboot is required for the new rules to take effect." >&2

    if ! grep -REq -- "$pattern" "$RULES_DIR"/*.rules 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$RULES_FILE"
    fi

    for code in -EACCES -EPERM; do
        ensure_rule "$arch" "$code"
    done
done

chown root:root "$RULES_FILE"
chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"

exit 0

#

chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if ! rpm --quiet -q grub2-common; then
    echo "grub2-common not installed; not applicable." >&2
    exit 0
fi

GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE="/etc/default/grub"
ARG_NAME="pti"
ARG_VALUE="on"
ARG="${ARG_NAME}=${ARG_VALUE}"

# --- Apply to all currently installed kernels via grubby ---
# Remove any existing pti= argument (e.g. pti=off) then set pti=on.
grubby --update-kernel=ALL --remove-args="${ARG_NAME}" --args="${ARG}"

# --- Persist in /etc/default/grub so newly installed kernels inherit it ---
if [ -f "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" ]; then
    if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"; then
        if grep -Eq "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*(\"|[[:space:]])${ARG_NAME}=[^[:space:]\"]*" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"; then
            # Replace existing pti=<something> with pti=on
            sed -i -E "s/(^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*)\b${ARG_NAME}=[^[:space:]\"]*/\1${ARG}/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
        else
            # Append pti=on inside the quoted string
            sed -i -E "s/^(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\")(.*)(\")$/\1\2 ${ARG}\3/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
            # Clean up possible double space if the value was empty
            sed -i -E "s/^(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\") +/\1/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
        fi
    else
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${ARG}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${ARG}\"" > "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
fi

echo "Kernel Page-Table Isolation (${ARG}) has been enabled for all installed kernels and will persist for future kernels."
exit 0

SYSCTL_VALUE="0"
PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-ipv6-router-advertisements.conf"

# Skip gracefully if IPv6 is entirely disabled on the system
if [[ ! -d /proc/sys/net/ipv6 ]]; then
    echo "IPv6 is disabled on this system; nothing to do."
    exit 0
fi

# Remove conflicting definitions from other sysctl configuration sources
# so our persistent file is authoritative.
CONF_SOURCES=(/etc/sysctl.conf)
for dir in /etc/sysctl.d /run/sysctl.d /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d /usr/lib/sysctl.d; do
    if [[ -d "$dir" ]]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            CONF_SOURCES+=("$f")
        done < <(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
    fi
done

for param in "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[@]}"; do
    param_regex="${param//./\\.}"
    for conf in "${CONF_SOURCES[@]}"; do
        [[ -f "$conf" ]] || continue
        [[ "$conf" == "$PERSIST_FILE" ]] && continue
        # Only touch writable regular files under /etc (leave vendor files in /usr alone
        # unless they conflict; comment out conflicting lines where possible)
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param_regex}[[:space:]]*=" "$conf"; then
            if [[ -w "$conf" ]]; then
                sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*(${param_regex}[[:space:]]*=.*)$/# \1/" "$conf"
            fi
        fi
    done
done

# Write the persistent configuration idempotently
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$PERSIST_FILE")"

desired_content="$(printf '%s = %s\n' "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[0]}" "$SYSCTL_VALUE"; printf '%s = %s\n' "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[1]}" "$SYSCTL_VALUE")"

if [[ ! -f "$PERSIST_FILE" ]] || [[ "$(cat "$PERSIST_FILE")" != "$desired_content" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$desired_content" > "$PERSIST_FILE"
fi

chmod 0644 "$PERSIST_FILE"
chown root:root "$PERSIST_FILE"

if command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    restorecon -F "$PERSIST_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# Apply the setting at runtime for the 'all' and 'default' keys
for param in "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[@]}"; do
    current="$(sysctl -n "$param" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
    if [[ "$current" != "$SYSCTL_VALUE" ]]; then
        sysctl -w "${param}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null
    fi
done

# Also apply immediately to every existing per-interface key so that
# interfaces already up stop accepting router advertisements right away.
for iface_path in /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/*/accept_ra; do
    [[ -e "$iface_path" ]] || continue
    current="$(cat "$iface_path" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
    if [[ "$current" != "$SYSCTL_VALUE" ]]; then
        echo "$SYSCTL_VALUE" > "$iface_path" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
done

echo "IPv6 router advertisement acceptance disabled (runtime and persistent)."
exit 0

PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-ipv6-router-advertisements.conf"

# Skip gracefully if IPv6 is entirely disabled on the system
if [[ ! -d /proc/sys/net/ipv6 ]]; then
    echo "IPv6 is disabled on this system; nothing to do."
    exit 0
fi

# Remove conflicting definitions from other sysctl configuration sources
# so our persistent file is authoritative.
CONF_SOURCES=(/etc/sysctl.conf)
for dir in /etc/sysctl.d /run/sysctl.d /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d /usr/lib/sysctl.d; do
    if [[ -d "$dir" ]]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            CONF_SOURCES+=("$f")
        done < <(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
    fi
done

for param in "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[@]}"; do
    param_regex="${param//./\\.}"
    for conf in "${CONF_SOURCES[@]}"; do
        [[ -f "$conf" ]] || continue
        [[ "$conf" == "$PERSIST_FILE" ]] && continue
        # Only touch writable regular files under /etc (leave vendor files in /usr alone
        # unless they conflict; comment out conflicting lines where possible)
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param_regex}[[:space:]]*=" "$conf"; then
            if [[ -w "$conf" ]]; then
                sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*(${param_regex}[[:space:]]*=.*)$/# \1/" "$conf"
            fi
        fi
    done
done

# Write the persistent configuration idempotently
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$PERSIST_FILE")"

desired_content="$(printf '%s = %s\n' "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[0]}" "$SYSCTL_VALUE"; printf '%s = %s\n' "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[1]}" "$SYSCTL_VALUE")"

if [[ ! -f "$PERSIST_FILE" ]] || [[ "$(cat "$PERSIST_FILE")" != "$desired_content" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$desired_content" > "$PERSIST_FILE"
fi

chmod 0644 "$PERSIST_FILE"
chown root:root "$PERSIST_FILE"

if command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    restorecon -F "$PERSIST_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# Apply the setting at runtime for the 'all' and 'default' keys
for param in "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[@]}"; do
    current="$(sysctl -n "$param" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
    if [[ "$current" != "$SYSCTL_VALUE" ]]; then
        sysctl -w "${param}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null
    fi
done

# Also apply immediately to every existing per-interface key so that
# interfaces already up stop accepting router advertisements right away.
for iface_path in /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/*/accept_ra; do
    [[ -e "$iface_path" ]] || continue
    current="$(cat "$iface_path" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
    if [[ "$current" != "$SYSCTL_VALUE" ]]; then
        echo "$SYSCTL_VALUE" > "$iface_path" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
done

echo "IPv6 router advertisement acceptance disabled (runtime and persistent)."
exit 0
```

        RULE="-a always,exit -F arch=${ARCH} -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${AUDIT_KEY}"
        grep -qF -- "$RULE" /etc/audit/audit.rules 2>/dev/null || echo "$RULE" >> /etc/audit/audit.rules
    done
    auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    echo "Audit rules for '${SYSCALL}' file-deletion tracking were already in place."

#

# so all sources are polled at a bounded maximum interval.
# Idempotent and persistent across reboots.

    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1

# Ensure chrony is installed
if ! rpm -q chrony >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum -y install chrony >/dev/null
fi

if [[ ! -f "$PRIMARY_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${PRIMARY_CONF} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Collect chrony configuration files to process (main config + drop-in dirs)
declare -a CONF_FILES=("$PRIMARY_CONF")
for dropdir in /etc/chrony.d /etc/chrony/conf.d; do
    if [[ -d "$dropdir" ]]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            CONF_FILES+=("$f")
        done < <(find "$dropdir" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
    fi
done

overall_changed=0

for conf in "${CONF_FILES[@]}"; do
    tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
    file_changed=0

    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
        # Match uncommented server/pool/peer directives lacking a maxpoll option
        if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*(server|pool|peer)[[:space:]]+[^[:space:]#] ]] \
           && ! [[ "$line" =~ (^|[[:space:]])maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+ ]]; then

                line="${BASH_REMATCH[1]} maxpoll ${MAXPOLL_VALUE} ${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
            else
                line="${line%"${line##*[![:space:]]}"} maxpoll ${MAXPOLL_VALUE}"
            fi
            file_changed=1
        fi
        printf '%s\n' "$line"
    done < "$conf" > "$tmpfile"

        # Preserve ownership, permissions, and SELinux context of the original
        cat "$tmpfile" > "$conf"
        overall_changed=1
    fi
    rm -f "$tmpfile"
done

if [[ "$overall_changed" -eq 1 ]]; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet chronyd.service; then
        systemctl restart chronyd.service
    else
        systemctl start chronyd.service || true
    fi
    echo "Remediation applied: maxpoll ${MAXPOLL_VALUE} added to time sources lacking it; chronyd reloaded."
else
    systemctl is-active --quiet chronyd.service || systemctl start chronyd.service || true
    echo "Already compliant: all configured time sources define an explicit maxpoll. No changes made."
fi

exit 0

/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/logout

touch "$RULES_FILE"
chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"

RULE_B32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
RULE_B64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"

rule_exists() {
    local arch="$1"
    grep -hE -- "^-a[[:space:]]+always,exit" "${RULES_DIR}"/*.rules 2>/dev/null | \
    grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+arch=${arch}\b" | \
    grep -E -- "(-S[[:space:]]+[^[:space:]]*\b${SYSCALL}\b|,${SYSCALL}\b|\b${SYSCALL},)" | \
    grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+auid>=1000" | \
    grep -qE -- "-F[[:space:]]+auid!=(unset|-1|4294967295)"
}

CHANGED=0

if ! rule_exists "b32"; then
    echo "$RULE_B32" >> "$RULES_FILE"
    CHANGED=1
fi

if ! rule_exists "b64"; then
    echo "$RULE_B64" >> "$RULES_FILE"
    CHANGED=1
fi

if [[ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]]; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load || true
    fi
    if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
    echo "Audit rules for '${SYSCALL}' added and loaded."
else
    echo "Audit rules for '${SYSCALL}' already present; no changes made."
fi

exit 0

set -o pipefail

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if ! grep -qi 'release 8' /etc/redhat-release 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Warning: this script is intended for RHEL 8 systems." >&2
fi

changed=0

# Ensure the crypto-policies and fips-mode-setup tooling is installed
if ! command -v fips-mode-setup >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum -y install crypto-policies-scripts || {
        echo "Failed to install crypto-policies-scripts." >&2
        exit 1
    }
fi

fips_setup_enabled=0
if fips-mode-setup --check 2>/dev/null | grep -qi 'FIPS mode is enabled'; then
    fips_setup_enabled=1
fi

# Enable FIPS mode if not already enabled (regenerates initramfs, sets kernel args, dracut config)
if [ "$fips_setup_enabled" -ne 1 ]; then
    fips-mode-setup --enable || {
        echo "fips-mode-setup --enable failed." >&2
        exit 1
    }
    changed=1
fi

# Ensure the system-wide crypto policy is FIPS (or a FIPS-based subpolicy)
current_policy="$(update-crypto-policies --show 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]')"
case "$current_policy" in
    FIPS|FIPS:*)
        : # already FIPS-based
        ;;
    *)
        update-crypto-policies --set FIPS || {
            echo "Failed to set system crypto policy to FIPS." >&2
            exit 1
        }
        changed=1
        ;;
esac

# Ensure fips=1 is present on the kernel command line for all installed kernels
if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^args=' | grep -qv 'fips=1'; then
        grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="fips=1" || {
            echo "Failed to add fips=1 kernel argument." >&2
            exit 1
        }
        changed=1
    fi
fi

# Ensure GRUB defaults carry fips=1 for future kernel installs
grub_default="/etc/default/grub"
if [ -f "$grub_default" ]; then
    if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$grub_default"; then
        if ! grep '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$grub_default" | grep -q 'fips=1'; then
            sed -i 's/^\(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="[^"]*\)"/\1 fips=1"/' "$grub_default"
            changed=1
        fi
    else
        echo 'GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="fips=1"' >> "$grub_default"
        changed=1
    fi
fi

# If /boot resides on a separate partition, ensure boot= kernel argument is set (required for FIPS integrity checks)
boot_dev="$(findmnt --noheadings --output SOURCE /boot 2>/dev/null)"
root_dev="$(findmnt --noheadings --output SOURCE / 2>/dev/null)"
if [ -n "$boot_dev" ] && [ "$boot_dev" != "$root_dev" ]; then
    boot_uuid="$(blkid -s UUID -o value "$boot_dev" 2>/dev/null)"
    if [ -n "$boot_uuid" ] && command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^args=' | grep -qv "boot=UUID=${boot_uuid}"; then
            grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="boot=UUID=${boot_uuid}" || true
            changed=1
        fi
    fi
fi

# Verify runtime state where possible
runtime_fips="$(cat /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"

if [ "$runtime_fips" -eq 1 ] && [ "$changed" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "System is already operating in FIPS mode with FIPS crypto policy. No changes made."
    exit 0
fi

if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    echo "FIPS mode configuration applied. A reboot is REQUIRED for FIPS mode to take full effect."
else
    echo "FIPS configuration is in place. A reboot may be required for the kernel to enter FIPS mode."
fi

exit 0

PATH_FILTER="-F path=/usr/sbin/unix_update"
PERM_FILTER="-F perm=x"
AUID_FILTERS="-F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset"
KEY="privileged-unix_update"
FULL_RULE="$ACTION_ARCH_FILTERS $PATH_FILTER $PERM_FILTER $AUID_FILTERS -k $KEY"

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
DEFAULT_FILE="$RULES_DIR/privileged.rules"

mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"
chmod 0750 "$RULES_DIR"

# Search all persistent rule files for an existing rule covering this path
matched_file=""
for f in "$RULES_DIR"/*.rules; do
    [ -e "$f" ] || continue
    if grep -Eq -- "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+always,exit[[:space:]].*-F[[:space:]]+path=/usr/sbin/unix_update([[:space:]]|$)" "$f"; then
        matched_file="$f"
        break
    fi
done

if [ -n "$matched_file" ]; then
    # A rule for this path exists; ensure it exactly matches the required rule
    if ! grep -Fxq -- "$FULL_RULE" "$matched_file"; then
        # Replace any line auditing execution of this path with the correct rule
        sed -i -E "\|^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+always,exit[[:space:]].*-F[[:space:]]+path=/usr/sbin/unix_update([[:space:]].*)?$|d" "$matched_file"
        echo "$FULL_RULE" >> "$matched_file"
    fi
    target_file="$matched_file"
else
    # No rule exists anywhere; add it to the default rules file
    touch "$DEFAULT_FILE"
    echo "$FULL_RULE" >> "$DEFAULT_FILE"
    target_file="$DEFAULT_FILE"
fi

chown root:root "$target_file"
chmod 0640 "$target_file"

# Also ensure legacy /etc/audit/audit.rules is regenerated / contains the rule
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
    if [ -f /etc/audit/audit.rules ] && ! grep -Fxq -- "$FULL_RULE" /etc/audit/audit.rules; then
        echo "$FULL_RULE" >> /etc/audit/audit.rules

        service auditd restart >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    fi

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then

    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd.service; then
        service auditd restart >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    else
        service auditd start >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl start auditd.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Enforce GNOME idle screen lock (lock-enabled=true) via dconf
# system database and lock the key so users cannot override it.
# Idempotent and persistent across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# If dconf is not present (no GNOME), there is nothing to remediate.
if ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "dconf not found; GNOME does not appear to be installed. Nothing to do."
    exit 0
fi

DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_PROFILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/user"
DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/locks"
SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver-lock"
LOCK_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-screensaver-lock"

CHANGED=0

# --- 1. Ensure the dconf user profile references the 'local' system database ---
mkdir -p "$DCONF_PROFILE_DIR"

if [[ ! -f "$DCONF_PROFILE" ]]; then
    printf 'user-db:user\nsystem-db:local\n' > "$DCONF_PROFILE"
    CHANGED=1
else
    if ! grep -qx 'user-db:user' "$DCONF_PROFILE"; then
        # user-db line must come first; prepend it.
        sed -i '1i user-db:user' "$DCONF_PROFILE"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
    if ! grep -qx 'system-db:local' "$DCONF_PROFILE"; then
        printf 'system-db:local\n' >> "$DCONF_PROFILE"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
fi

# --- 2. Ensure the system database directories exist ---
mkdir -p "$DCONF_DB_DIR" "$DCONF_LOCKS_DIR"

# --- 3. Neutralize any conflicting lock-enabled=false in other local.d keyfiles ---
while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
    [[ "$f" == "$SETTINGS_FILE" ]] && continue
    if grep -Eq '^\s*lock-enabled\s*=\s*false\s*$' "$f"; then
        sed -ri 's/^\s*(lock-enabled\s*=\s*false\s*)$/# \1/' "$f"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done < <(find "$DCONF_DB_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type f -print0 2>/dev/null)

# --- 4. Write the enforced setting (only if content differs) ---
SETTINGS_CONTENT='[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
'
if [[ ! -f "$SETTINGS_FILE" ]] || [[ "$(cat "$SETTINGS_FILE")" != "$(printf '%s' "$SETTINGS_CONTENT")" ]]; then
    printf '%s' "$SETTINGS_CONTENT" > "$SETTINGS_FILE"
    CHANGED=1
fi

# --- 5. Lock the key so individual users cannot override it ---
LOCK_CONTENT='/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
'
if [[ ! -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]] || [[ "$(cat "$LOCK_FILE")" != "$(printf '%s' "$LOCK_CONTENT")" ]]; then
    printf '%s' "$LOCK_CONTENT" > "$LOCK_FILE"
    CHANGED=1
fi

# --- 6. Sane ownership/permissions on managed files ---
chown root:root "$DCONF_PROFILE" "$SETTINGS_FILE" "$LOCK_FILE"
chmod 0644 "$DCONF_PROFILE" "$SETTINGS_FILE" "$LOCK_FILE"

# --- 7. Compile the dconf databases so the policy takes effect ---
# Also recompile if the binary database is missing or stale.
if [[ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]] || [[ ! -f /etc/dconf/db/local ]] || \
   [[ "$SETTINGS_FILE" -nt /etc/dconf/db/local ]] || \
   [[ "$LOCK_FILE" -nt /etc/dconf/db/local ]]; then
    dconf update
    echo "Remediation applied: GNOME screensaver lock-enabled enforced and locked."
else
    echo "Already compliant: GNOME screensaver lock-enabled is enforced and locked."
fi

exit 0

        already_configured=1
        break

        break

    changed=1

    echo "Remediation applied: rsyslog now forwards all logs to ${LOGHOST}."
else
    echo "System already compliant: rsyslog forwards all logs to ${LOGHOST}."
fi

exit 0

LOCK_FILE="${LOCKS_DIR}/banner-message"

    if [ ! -d "$d" ]; then

        changed=1
    fi

    fi

desired_banner="[org/gnome/login-screen]
banner-message-enable=true
banner-message-text='${BANNER_TEXT}'
"

/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text
"

if [ ! -f "$LOCK_FILE" ] || [ "$(cat "$LOCK_FILE")" != "$(printf '%s' "$desired_lock")" ]; then
    printf '%s' "$desired_lock" > "$LOCK_FILE"
    changed=1
fi

chown root:root "$PROFILE_FILE" "$BANNER_FILE" "$LOCK_FILE"

fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -u

SERVICE="kdump.service"

# If systemd is not present, nothing to do
if ! command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "systemctl not found; cannot manage ${SERVICE}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Only act if the unit exists (installed) or is referenced at all
unit_known() {
    systemctl list-unit-files "${SERVICE}" --no-legend 2>/dev/null | grep -q "${SERVICE}" \
    || systemctl status "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

if ! unit_known; then
    echo "${SERVICE} is not installed; ensuring it is masked as a preventive measure."
    systemctl mask "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    exit 0
fi

# Stop the service if it is currently running
if systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE}"; then
    echo "Stopping ${SERVICE}..."
    systemctl stop "${SERVICE}"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already stopped."
fi

# Disable the service so it does not start at boot
if systemctl is-enabled --quiet "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Disabling ${SERVICE}..."
    systemctl disable "${SERVICE}"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already disabled."
fi

# Mask the service to block manual or dependency-based activation
current_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ "${current_state}" != "masked" ]; then
    echo "Masking ${SERVICE}..."
    systemctl mask "${SERVICE}"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already masked."
fi

systemctl daemon-reload

# Verify final state
final_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE}"; then
    echo "ERROR: ${SERVICE} is still active." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ "${final_state}" != "masked" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${SERVICE} is not masked (state: ${final_state})." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Remediation complete: ${SERVICE} is stopped, disabled, and masked."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

FSTAB="/etc/fstab"
MOUNT_POINT="/dev/shm"
REQUIRED_OPT="nodev"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if grep -Eqs "^[[:space:]]*[^#[:space:]]+[[:space:]]+${MOUNT_POINT}[[:space:]]" "$FSTAB"; then
    current_line=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*[^#[:space:]]+[[:space:]]+${MOUNT_POINT}[[:space:]]" "$FSTAB" | head -n1)
    current_opts=$(echo "$current_line" | awk '{print $4}')
    if ! echo "$current_opts" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -qx "$REQUIRED_OPT"; then
        if [ "$current_opts" = "defaults" ]; then
            new_opts="defaults,${REQUIRED_OPT}"
        else
            new_opts="${current_opts},${REQUIRED_OPT}"
        fi
        awk -v mp="$MOUNT_POINT" -v newopts="$new_opts" '
            $0 !~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ && $2 == mp && !done {
                $4 = newopts
                done = 1
                print $1"\t"$2"\t"$3"\t"$4"\t"$5"\t"$6
                next
            }
            { print }
        ' "$FSTAB" > "${FSTAB}.tmp" && cat "${FSTAB}.tmp" > "$FSTAB" && rm -f "${FSTAB}.tmp"
    fi
else
    printf 'tmpfs\t%s\ttmpfs\tdefaults,%s\t0\t0\n' "$MOUNT_POINT" "$REQUIRED_OPT" >> "$FSTAB"
fi

if findmnt --kernel "$MOUNT_POINT" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    active_opts=$(findmnt --kernel -n -o OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT")
    if ! echo "$active_opts" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -qx "$REQUIRED_OPT"; then
        mount -o "remount,${REQUIRED_OPT}" "$MOUNT_POINT"
    fi
else
    mount "$MOUNT_POINT"
fi

exit 0

[[ -f /etc/sysctl.conf ]] && conf_files+=("/etc/sysctl.conf")
for d in /etc/sysctl.d /usr/lib/sysctl.d /run/sysctl.d; do
    if [[ -d "$d" ]]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            conf_files+=("$f")

    if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$f"; then

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

PARAM="net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects"
VALUE="0"
PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-ipv6-redirects.conf"

if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Skip gracefully if IPv6 is not present on the system
if [[ ! -d /proc/sys/net/ipv6 ]]; then
    echo "IPv6 is not enabled on this system; no remediation required."
    exit 0
fi

# --- Runtime remediation (idempotent) ---
current="$(sysctl -n "$PARAM" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
if [[ "$current" != "$VALUE" ]]; then
    sysctl -w "${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null
    echo "Runtime: set ${PARAM} to ${VALUE}."
else
    echo "Runtime: ${PARAM} already set to ${VALUE}."
fi

# --- Remove conflicting persistent entries in standard sysctl locations ---
conf_files=()
[[ -f /etc/sysctl.conf ]] && conf_files+=("/etc/sysctl.conf")
for d in /etc/sysctl.d /usr/lib/sysctl.d /run/sysctl.d; do
    if [[ -d "$d" ]]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            conf_files+=("$f")
        done < <(find "$d" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0)
    fi
done

for f in "${conf_files[@]}"; do
    [[ "$f" == "$PERSIST_FILE" ]] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$f"; then
        if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*${VALUE}\s*$" "$f" && \
           ! grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*[^0[:space:]]" "$f"; then
            continue
        fi
        sed -ri "s|^\s*(${PARAM}\s*=).*|# Remediated: setting moved to ${PERSIST_FILE}\n# \1 (previous value removed)|" "$f"
        echo "Cleaned conflicting entry for ${PARAM} in ${f}."
    fi
done

# --- Persistent remediation (idempotent) ---
if [[ -f "$PERSIST_FILE" ]] && grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*${VALUE}\s*$" "$PERSIST_FILE"; then
    echo "Persistence: ${PERSIST_FILE} already contains correct setting."
else
    if [[ -f "$PERSIST_FILE" ]] && grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$PERSIST_FILE"; then
        sed -ri "s|^\s*${PARAM}\s*=.*|${PARAM} = ${VALUE}|" "$PERSIST_FILE"
    else
        printf '%s = %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$PERSIST_FILE"
    fi
    chmod 0644 "$PERSIST_FILE"
    chown root:root "$PERSIST_FILE"
    echo "Persistence: ensured ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} in ${PERSIST_FILE}."
fi

# --- Reload sysctl settings to confirm applied configuration ---
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# --- Verify final state ---
final="$(sysctl -n "$PARAM")"
if [[ "$final" == "$VALUE" ]]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${PARAM} is set to ${VALUE} at runtime and persistently."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: ${PARAM} is '${final}', expected '${VALUE}'." >&2
    exit 1
fi
```

else

    grep -qx 'system-db:local' "$DCONF_PROFILE_FILE" || printf 'system-db:local\n' >> "$DCONF_PROFILE_FILE"
fi
chmod 0644 "$DCONF_PROFILE_FILE"

# Ensure lock-enabled=true is set under [org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
ensure_setting() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        printf '[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]\nlock-enabled=true\n' > "$file"
        return
    fi
    if grep -q '^\[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver\]' "$file"; then
        # Check if lock-enabled=true already exists within the section
        if awk '
            /^\[/{insec=0}
            /^\[org\/gnome\/desktop\/screensaver\]/{insec=1; next}
            insec && /^[[:space:]]*lock-enabled[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*true[[:space:]]*$/{found=1}
            END{exit found?0:1}
        ' "$file"; then
            return
        fi
        # Remove any existing lock-enabled lines in the section, then add correct one
        awk '
            /^\[/{insec=($0=="[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]")}
            insec && /^[[:space:]]*lock-enabled[[:space:]]*=/{next}
            {print}
            /^\[org\/gnome\/desktop\/screensaver\]$/{print "lock-enabled=true"}
        ' "$file" > "${file}.tmp" && mv "${file}.tmp" "$file"
    else
        printf '\n[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]\nlock-enabled=true\n' >> "$file"
    fi
}

ensure_setting "$SETTINGS_FILE"
chmod 0644 "$SETTINGS_FILE"

# Ensure the setting is locked so users cannot override it
if [ ! -f "$LOCKS_FILE" ] || ! grep -qx '/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled' "$LOCKS_FILE"; then
    printf '/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled\n' >> "$LOCKS_FILE"
fi
chmod 0644 "$LOCKS_FILE"

# Apply the configuration to the dconf system databases
dconf update

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONF_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/tipc.conf"
MODULE="tipc"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d

# Ensure "install tipc /bin/false" directive is present
if ! grep -Pqs '^\s*install\s+tipc\s+(/bin/false|/bin/true)\b' "$CONF_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "install ${MODULE} /bin/false" >> "$CONF_FILE"
fi

# Ensure the module is blacklisted
if ! grep -Pqs '^\s*blacklist\s+tipc\b' "$CONF_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "blacklist ${MODULE}" >> "$CONF_FILE"
fi

# Set secure ownership and permissions on the config file
chown root:root "$CONF_FILE"
chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"

# Unload the module if it is currently loaded
if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "${MODULE}"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || rmmod "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Verify remediation state
if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "${MODULE}"; then
    echo "WARNING: ${MODULE} module is still loaded; a reboot may be required." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Remediation complete: ${MODULE} module is disabled, blacklisted, and not loaded."
exit 0
