#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONF_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/tipc.conf"
MODULE="tipc"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d

# Ensure "install tipc /bin/false" directive is present
if ! grep -Pqs '^\s*install\s+tipc\s+(/bin/false|/bin/true)\b' "$CONF_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "install ${MODULE} /bin/false" >> "$CONF_FILE"
fi

# Ensure the module is blacklisted
if ! grep -Pqs '^\s*blacklist\s+tipc\b' "$CONF_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "blacklist ${MODULE}" >> "$CONF_FILE"
fi

# Set secure ownership and permissions on the config file
chown root:root "$CONF_FILE"
chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"

# Unload the module if it is currently loaded
if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "${MODULE}"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || rmmod "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Verify remediation state
if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "${MODULE}"; then
    echo "WARNING: ${MODULE} module is still loaded; a reboot may be required." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Remediation complete: ${MODULE} module is disabled, blacklisted, and not loaded."
exit 0
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disable reboot on Ctrl-Alt-Del key sequence (RHEL 8)
# Masks the systemd ctrl-alt-del.target so the key sequence has no effect.
# The mask lives in /etc/systemd/system, so it persists across reboots
# and is not overwritten by package/system updates.
# Also disables the Ctrl-Alt-Del "burst" action in systemd configuration.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

# --- Must be root ---
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

CHANGED=0

# --- 1. Mask ctrl-alt-del.target (persists in /etc/systemd/system) ---
TARGET="ctrl-alt-del.target"

current_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "${TARGET}" 2>/dev/null || true)"

if [[ "${current_state}" != "masked" ]]; then
    echo "Masking ${TARGET} ..."
    systemctl mask --now "${TARGET}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
        # Fallback: create the mask symlink manually
        ln -sf /dev/null "/etc/systemd/system/${TARGET}"
    }
    CHANGED=1
else
    echo "${TARGET} is already masked."
fi

# Verify mask symlink points at /dev/null
MASK_LINK="/etc/systemd/system/${TARGET}"
if [[ "$(readlink -f "${MASK_LINK}" 2>/dev/null)" != "/dev/null" ]]; then
    echo "Ensuring mask symlink ${MASK_LINK} -> /dev/null ..."
    ln -sf /dev/null "${MASK_LINK}"
    CHANGED=1
fi

# --- 2. Disable Ctrl-Alt-Del burst action in systemd system.conf ---
SYSTEMD_CONF="/etc/systemd/system.conf"
PARAM="CtrlAltDelBurstAction"
VALUE="none"

if [[ ! -f "${SYSTEMD_CONF}" ]]; then
    touch "${SYSTEMD_CONF}"
fi

if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "${SYSTEMD_CONF}"; then
    if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${SYSTEMD_CONF}"; then
        echo "Correcting ${PARAM} in ${SYSTEMD_CONF} ..."
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=.*|${PARAM}=${VALUE}|" "${SYSTEMD_CONF}"
        CHANGED=1
    else
        echo "${PARAM}=${VALUE} already set in ${SYSTEMD_CONF}."
    fi
else
    echo "Adding ${PARAM}=${VALUE} to ${SYSTEMD_CONF} ..."
    printf '%s=%s\n' "${PARAM}" "${VALUE}" >> "${SYSTEMD_CONF}"
    CHANGED=1
fi

# --- 3. Reload systemd if anything changed ---
if [[ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo "Reloading systemd daemon configuration ..."
    systemctl daemon-reload
fi

# --- 4. Verify final state ---
final_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "${TARGET}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [[ "${final_state}" == "masked" ]]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot sequence is disabled (${TARGET} is masked)."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: ${TARGET} is not masked (state: ${final_state})." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disable vsyscall (legacy virtual system calls) on RHEL 8
# Adds 'vsyscall=none' to the kernel command line for all installed kernels
# via grubby, and to /etc/default/grub so future kernels inherit the setting.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

KERNEL_ARG_KEY="vsyscall"
KERNEL_ARG_VALUE="none"
KERNEL_ARG="${KERNEL_ARG_KEY}=${KERNEL_ARG_VALUE}"
GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE="/etc/default/grub"

# --- Must be root ---
if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# --- Ensure grubby is available ---
if ! command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: grubby command not found; cannot remediate." >&2
    exit 1
fi

changed=0

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Apply to all currently installed kernels via grubby
#    (grubby replaces an existing vsyscall=<value> with the new value)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
needs_grubby_update=0
while IFS= read -r line; do
    case "${line}" in
        args=*)
            if ! grep -qE "(^|[\" ])${KERNEL_ARG_KEY}=${KERNEL_ARG_VALUE}([\" ]|\$)" <<< "${line}"; then
                needs_grubby_update=1
            fi
            ;;
    esac
done < <(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null)

if [[ "${needs_grubby_update}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="${KERNEL_ARG}"
    changed=1
    echo "Applied '${KERNEL_ARG}' to all installed kernels via grubby."
else
    echo "All installed kernels already have '${KERNEL_ARG}'."
fi

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Persist in /etc/default/grub (GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX) so future kernels
#    and regenerated grub configs inherit the setting
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ ! -f "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" ]]; then
    touch "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
fi

if grep -qE '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"; then
    current_line="$(grep -E '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" | tail -n 1)"

    if grep -qE "(^|[\" ])${KERNEL_ARG_KEY}=${KERNEL_ARG_VALUE}([\" ]|\$)" <<< "${current_line}"; then

        # Append the argument inside the existing quoted value
        sed -ri "s/^(\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\")(.*)\"/\1\2 ${KERNEL_ARG}\"/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
        # Handle unquoted or empty variants
        sed -ri "s/^(\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=)\$/\1\"${KERNEL_ARG}\"/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
        # Clean up any accidental leading space inside quotes
        sed -ri 's/^(\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=")\s+/\1/' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
        changed=1
        echo "Appended '${KERNEL_ARG}' to GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX in ${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}."
    fi
else
    printf 'GRUB_CMDLIN

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

FSTAB="/etc/fstab"
MOUNT_POINT="/dev/shm"
REQUIRED_OPT="nosuid"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# --- Persist configuration in /etc/fstab ---

if grep -Eq "^[^#]\S*[[:space:]]+${MOUNT_POINT}[[:space:]]" "$FSTAB" 2>/dev/null || \
   grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*[^#[:space:]]+[[:space:]]+${MOUNT_POINT}[[:space:]]" "$FSTAB" 2>/dev/null; then
    # An fstab entry for /dev/shm exists; ensure it includes nosuid
    if ! awk -v mp="$MOUNT_POINT" -v opt="$REQUIRED_OPT" '
        $0 !~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ && $2 == mp {
            n = split($4, opts, ",")
            for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) if (opts[i] == opt) found = 1
        }
        END { exit found ? 0 : 1 }
    ' "$FSTAB"; then
        cp -p "$FSTAB" "${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
        awk -v mp="$MOUNT_POINT" -v opt="$REQUIRED_OPT" '
            $0 !~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ && $2 == mp {
                n = split($4, opts, ",")
                found = 0
                for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) if (opts[i] == opt) found = 1
                if (!found) {
                    if ($4 == "" || $4 == "defaults") { $4 = "defaults," opt }
                    else { $4 = $4 "," opt }
                }
                print $1 "\t" $2 "\t" $3 "\t" $4 "\t" (NF >= 5 ? $5 : 0) "\t" (NF >= 6 ? $6 : 0)
                next
            }
            { print }
        ' "$FSTAB" > "${FSTAB}.tmp"
        mv "${FSTAB}.tmp" "$FSTAB"
        chmod 644 "$FSTAB"
        echo "Updated existing ${MOUNT_POINT} entry in ${FSTAB} with ${REQUIRED_OPT}."
    else
        echo "${FSTAB} entry for ${MOUNT_POINT} already includes ${REQUIRED_OPT}."
    fi
else
    cp -p "$FSTAB" "${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
    printf 'tmpfs\t%s\ttmpfs\tdefaults,nodev,noexec,nosuid\t0\t0\n' "$MOUNT_POINT" >> "$FSTAB"
    echo "Added ${MOUNT_POINT} entry with ${REQUIRED_OPT} to ${FSTAB}."
fi

# --- Apply immediately to the live mount ---

if findmnt --kernel "$MOUNT_POINT" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_OPTS="$(findmnt --kernel -n -o OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT")"
    if echo "$CURRENT_OPTS" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -qx "$REQUIRED_OPT"; then
        echo "${MOUNT_POINT} is already mounted with ${REQUIRED_OPT}."
    else
        mount -o "remount,${REQUIRED_OPT}" "$MOUNT_POINT"
        echo "Remounted ${MOUNT_POINT} with ${REQUIRED_OPT}."
    fi
else
    mount "$MOUNT_POINT"
    echo "Mounted ${MOUNT_POINT} using ${FSTAB} options."
fi

# --- Verify ---

if findmnt --kernel -n -o OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -qx "$REQUIRED_OPT"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${MOUNT_POINT} is mounted with ${REQUIRED_OPT} and configured persistently."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to enforce ${REQUIRED_OPT} on ${MOUNT_POINT}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
PARAM="IgnoreUserKnownHosts"
VALUE="yes"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

cp -p "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

if grep -Eqi "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}([[:space:]]|=)" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}([[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*).*/${PARAM} ${VALUE}/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    if grep -Eqi "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}([[:space:]]|=)" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        sed -ri "0,/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}([[:space:]]|=).*/s//${PARAM} ${VALUE}/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    else
        printf "\n%s %s\n" "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

if [ -d /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d ]; then
    for f in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -Eqi "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}([[:space:]]|=)" "$f"; then
            sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}([[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*).*/${PARAM} ${VALUE}/I" "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

chown root:root "$SSHD_CONFIG"
chmod 0600 "$SSHD_CONFIG"

if ! sshd -t; then
    echo "Error: sshd configuration test failed. Review $SSHD_CONFIG." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
    systemctl restart sshd
else
    systemctl enable --now sshd || true
fi

echo "Remediation complete: ${PARAM} is set to ${VALUE}."
exit 0

set -euo pipefail

MAX_DAYS=365
SHADOW_FILE="/etc/shadow"
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"

if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$SHADOW_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: $SHADOW_FILE not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# --- Ensure PASS_MAX_DAYS in /etc/login.defs so new accounts are compliant ---
if [[ -f "$LOGIN_DEFS" ]]; then
    current_defs="$(awk '!/^\s*#/ && $1 == "PASS_MAX_DAYS" {val=$2} END {print val}' "$LOGIN_DEFS")"
    if [[ -z "${current_defs}" ]]; then
        printf 'PASS_MAX_DAYS %s\n' "$MAX_DAYS" >> "$LOGIN_DEFS"
        echo "Added PASS_MAX_DAYS $MAX_DAYS to $LOGIN_DEFS"
    elif ! [[ "$current_defs" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || (( current_defs > MAX_DAYS )) || (( current_defs < 1 )); then
        sed -ri "s/^\s*PASS_MAX_DAYS\s+\S+.*$/PASS_MAX_DAYS ${MAX_DAYS}/" "$LOGIN_DEFS"
        echo "Updated PASS_MAX_DAYS to $MAX_DAYS in $LOGIN_DEFS"
    fi
fi

# --- Remediate all existing local accounts in /etc/shadow ---
changed=0
noncompliant_users=()

while IFS=: read -r username _pass _lastchg _min max _rest; do
    [[ -z "$username" ]] && continue
    if [[ -z "$max" ]] || ! [[ "$max" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || (( max > MAX_DAYS )) || (( max < 1 )); then
        noncompliant_users+=("$username")
    fi
done < "$SHADOW_FILE"

for user in "${noncompliant_users[@]:-}"; do
    [[ -z "$user" ]] && continue
    if chage --maxdays "$MAX_DAYS" "$user"; then
        echo "Remediated: set maximum password age to $MAX_DAYS days for user '$user'"
        changed=1
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to set maximum password age for user '$user'" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

if (( changed == 0 )); then
    echo "All local user accounts already comply with the ${MAX_DAYS}-day maximum password lifetime."
else
    echo "Remediation complete. All local user accounts now comply with the ${MAX_DAYS}-day maximum password lifetime."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/audit.rules"
COMMAND_PATH="/usr/sbin/postqueue"
AUDIT_KEY="privileged-mail"
REQUIRED_RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${COMMAND_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

# Ensure rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Ensure rules file exists
if [ ! -f "${RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${RULES_FILE}"
    chmod 0600 "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Build a regex that matches an existing rule for this path regardless of
# field ordering or key naming, so we can normalize it idempotently.
rule_exists=0
while IFS= read -r existing_file; do
    if grep -Eq -- "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+always,exit[[:space:]].*-F[[:space:]]+path=${COMMAND_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)" "${existing_file}"; then
        # A rule for this path already exists; verify it matches required form
        if grep -Fxq -- "${REQUIRED_RULE}" "${existing_file}"; then
            rule_exists=1
        else
            # Replace non-conforming rule(s) for this path with the required rule
            tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
            grep -Ev -- "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+always,exit[[:space:]].*-F[[:space:]]+path=${COMMAND_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)" "${existing_file}" > "${tmpfile}" || true
            cat "${tmpfile}" > "${existing_file}"
            rm -f "${tmpfile}"
        fi
    fi
done < <(find "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.rules' 2>/dev/null)

# Append the required rule if not already present in exact form anywhere
if [ "${rule_exists}" -ne 1 ]; then
    if ! grep -Fxq -- "${REQUIRED_RULE}" "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"/*.rules 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${REQUIRED_RULE}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# Ensure sane permissions on the rules file
chmod 0600 "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true

# Load rules via augenrules so they take effect now and persist across reboot
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
else
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

exit 0

    exit 1

CONF_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/firewire-core.conf"
INSTALL_LINE="install ${MODULE} /bin/false"
BLACKLIST_LINE="blacklist ${MODULE}"

mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d

if [ -f "$CONF_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -Eq "^\s*install\s+${MODULE}\s+" "$CONF_FILE"; then
        if ! grep -Eq "^\s*install\s+${MODULE}\s+/bin/false\s*$" "$CONF_FILE"; then

        echo "$INSTALL_LINE" >> "$CONF_FILE"

    if ! grep -Eq "^\s*blacklist\s+${MODULE}\s*$" "$CONF_FILE"; then
        echo "$BLACKLIST_LINE" >> "$CONF_FILE"
    fi
else
    {
        echo "$INSTALL_LINE"
        echo "$BLACKLIST_LINE"
    } > "$CONF_FILE"
fi

chown root:root "$CONF_FILE"
chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"

if lsmod | grep -q "^firewire_core"; then
    modprobe -r "$MODULE" 2>/dev/null || rmmod "$MODULE" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if command -v dracut >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dracut -f 2>/dev/null || true
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
PARAM="X11UseLocalhost"
VALUE="yes"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $SSHD_CONFIG not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Remediate any drop-in include files that may set the parameter incorrectly
if [ -d /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d ]; then
    for f in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -Eqi "^\s*${PARAM}\b" "$f"; then
            sed -ri "s/^\s*(${PARAM})\s+.*/\1 ${VALUE}/I" "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Enforce the setting in the main sshd_config
if grep -Eqi "^\s*${PARAM}\b" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    # Normalize any existing (possibly incorrect) active directive
    sed -ri "s/^\s*(${PARAM})\s+.*/\1 ${VALUE}/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    if grep -Eqi "^\s*#\s*${PARAM}\b" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        # Insert the directive after the first commented occurrence
        sed -ri "0,/^\s*#\s*${PARAM}\b.*/s//&\n${PARAM} ${VALUE}/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    else
        printf '\n%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

# Verify configuration syntax before applying
if ! sshd -t; then
    echo "ERROR: sshd configuration validation failed; not restarting sshd." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Confirm effective runtime value
EFFECTIVE="$(sshd -T 2>/dev/null | awk 'tolower($1)=="x11uselocalhost"{print tolower($2)}')"
if [ "$EFFECTIVE" != "yes" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Effective ${PARAM} is '${EFFECTIVE}', expected 'yes'." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Reload/restart sshd to apply and persist behavior
if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
    systemctl restart sshd
fi
systemctl enable sshd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo "OK: ${PARAM} is set to '${VALUE}' and enforced in ${SSHD_CONFIG}."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Audit unsuccessful file open attempts (EACCES/EPERM)
# by users with auid >= 1000 (excluding unset), key=access.
# Covers both b32 and b64 syscall interfaces. Idempotent.

set -u

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/30-access.rules"

# Ensure rules directory exists
mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"

# Ensure rules file exists with sane permissions
if [ ! -f "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
    touch "$RULES_FILE"
fi
chown root:root "$RULES_FILE"
chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"

# Desired audit rules
declare -a RULES=(
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=access"
)

changed=0

# Helper: check if an equivalent rule already exists anywhere in rules.d
rule_exists() {
    local arch="$1"
    local exitval="$2"
    grep -rhsE -- "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)" "$RULES_DIR"/*.rules 2>/dev/null \
        | grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+arch=${arch}\b" \
        | grep -E -- "(-S[[:space:]]+[^ ]*\bopen\b|[,]open\b|\bopen,)" \
        | grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+exit=-${exitval}\b" \
        | grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+auid>=1000\b" \
        | grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+auid!=(unset|4294967295|-1)\b" \
        | grep -qE -- "(-F[[:space:]]+key=access\b|-k[[:space:]]+access\b)"
}

for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    arch=$(echo "$rule" | grep -oE 'arch=b(32|64)' | cut -d= -f2)
    exitval=$(echo "$rule" | grep -oE 'exit=-E[A-Z]+' | sed 's/exit=-//')
    if ! rule_exists "$arch" "$exitval"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$RULES_FILE"
        changed=1
    fi
done

# Load rules so they take effect now and persist across reboots
if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ] || ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q -- "key=access"; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    else
        auditctl -R "$RULES_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

# Ensure auditd is enabled to start at boot
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
BANNER_PATH="/etc/issue"

if [ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found. Is openssh-server installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Remove any commented-out Banner directives to avoid confusion, then
# ensure exactly one active 'Banner /etc/issue' directive exists.
if grep -Eq '^\s*Banner\s+' "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    # An active Banner directive exists; normalize it to the required value.
    sed -ri "s|^\s*Banner\s+.*|Banner ${BANNER_PATH}|" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    # No active directive; append one.
    printf '\nBanner %s\n' "$BANNER_PATH" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

# Deduplicate in case multiple active Banner lines existed.
if [ "$(grep -Ec '^\s*Banner\s+' "$SSHD_CONFIG")" -gt 1 ]; then
    awk -v banner="Banner ${BANNER_PATH}" '
        /^[[:space:]]*Banner[[:space:]]+/ {
            if (!seen) { print banner; seen=1 }
            next
        }
        { print }
    ' "$SSHD_CONFIG" > "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    chmod 600 "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    chown root:root "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    mv "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

# Restore SELinux context if applicable.
if command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    restorecon "$SSHD_CONFIG" || true
fi

# Reload sshd to apply the change immediately; config persists across reboots.
if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
    systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
fi

exit 0

    awk -v banner="Banner ${BANNER_PATH}" '
        /^[[:space:]]*Banner[[:space:]]+/ {
            if (!seen) { print banner; seen=1 }
            next
        }
        { print }
    ' "$SSHD_CONFIG" > "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    chmod 600 "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    chown root:root "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    mv "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

# Restore SELinux context if applicable.
if command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    restorecon "$SSHD_CONFIG" || true
fi

# Reload sshd to apply the change immediately; config persists across reboots.
if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
    systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -u

PARAM="net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_source_route"
VALUE="0"
SYSCTL_DROPIN="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-source-route.conf"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# --- Apply the setting at runtime ---
current_runtime="$(sysctl -n "${PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
if [ "${current_runtime}" != "${VALUE}" ]; then
    sysctl -w "${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null
    echo "Runtime: set ${PARAM} to ${VALUE}"
else
    echo "Runtime: ${PARAM} already set to ${VALUE}"
fi

# --- Remove or correct conflicting persistent entries ---
escaped_param="$(printf '%s' "${PARAM}" | sed 's/\./\\./g')"

conf_files=()
[ -f /etc/sysctl.conf ] && conf_files+=("/etc/sysctl.conf")
for d in /etc/sysctl.d /run/sysctl.d /usr/lib/sysctl.d /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d; do
    if [ -d "$d" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            conf_files+=("$f")
        done < <(find "$d" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
    fi
done

for f in "${conf_files[@]}"; do
    [ "$f" = "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${escaped_param}\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        if grep -Eq "^\s*${escaped_param}\s*=\s*${VALUE}\s*(#.*)?$" "$f" && \
           ! grep -Eq "^\s*${escaped_param}\s*=\s*[^0[:space:]]" "$f"; then
            continue
        fi
        sed -ri "s/^\s*(${escaped_param}\s*=.*)$/# \1  # disabled by remediation/" "$f"
        echo "Persistent: commented out conflicting entry in $f"
    fi
done

# --- Ensure the correct persistent setting exists in the drop-in file ---
if [ -f "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}" ] && grep -Eq "^\s*${escaped_param}\s*=\s*${VALUE}\s*$" "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}"; then
    echo "Persistent: ${PARAM} already configured in ${SYSCTL_DROPIN}"
else
    if [ -f "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}" ]; then
        sed -ri "/^\s*${escaped_param}\s*=/d" "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}"
    fi
    printf '%s = %s\n' "${PARAM}" "${VALUE}" >> "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}"
    chmod 0644 "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}"
    chown root:root "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}"
    echo "Persistent: wrote ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} to ${SYSCTL_DROPIN}"
fi

# --- Reload sysctl settings and flush routes ---
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
sysctl -w net.ipv4.route.flush=1 >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# --- Verify ---
final="$(sysctl -n "${PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
if [ "${final}" = "${VALUE}" ]; then
    echo "Remediation successful: ${PARAM} = ${final}"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Remediation FAILED: ${PARAM} = ${final}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disable chrony daemon network management (command) port on RHEL 8.
# Ensures "cmdport 0" is set in /etc/chrony.conf so chronyd does not open or
# listen on its management/command network socket (UDP 323).
# Idempotent and persistent across reboots (setting lives in the config file).
#

set -euo pipefail

CHRONY_CONF="/etc/chrony.conf"
CHANGED=0

# Ensure the configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "$CHRONY_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$CHRONY_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$CHRONY_CONF"
    CHANGED=1
fi

fix_file() {
    local file="$1"

    # If any active (uncommented) cmdport directive exists...
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*cmdport[[:space:]]+' "$file"; then
        # ...and it is not already exactly "cmdport 0", rewrite it.
        if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*cmdport[[:space:]]+0[[:space:]]*$' "$file"; then
            sed -ri 's/^[[:space:]]*cmdport[[:space:]]+.*/cmdport 0/' "$file"
            CHANGED=1
        fi
    else
        # No active directive present in this file: only append to main conf.
        if [ "$file" = "$CHRONY_CONF" ]; then
            printf '\ncmdport 0\n' >> "$file"
            CHANGED=1
        fi
    fi
}

# Fix main configuration file
fix_file "$CHRONY_CONF"

# Also neutralize any conflicting cmdport settings in drop-in config fragments
for dropin_dir in /etc/chrony.d /etc/chrony/conf.d; do
    if [ -d "$dropin_dir" ]; then
        for f in "$dropin_dir"/*.conf; do
            [ -f "$f" ] || continue
            if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*cmdport[[:space:]]+' "$f" \
               && ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*cmdport[[:space:]]+0[[:space:]]*$' "$f"; then
                sed -ri 's/^[[:space:]]*cmdport[[:space:]]+.*/cmdport 0/' "$f"
                CHANGED=1
            fi
        done
    fi
done

# Apply the change at runtime by restarting chronyd, but only if we modified
# the configuration and the service is currently active.
if [ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if systemctl is-active --quiet chronyd 2>/dev/null; then
            systemctl restart chronyd
        fi
    fi
    echo "Remediation applied: chrony cmdport set to 0 (management port disabled)."
else
    echo "Already compliant: chrony cmdport is set to 0."
fi

exit 0

set -u

AUDIT_BIN_PATH="/usr/sbin/setsebool"
KEY="privileged"
RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/privileged.rules"
RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${AUDIT_BIN_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

rule_present() {
    # Look through all persistent rules.d files for an equivalent rule
    local f
    for f in "${RULES_DIR}"/*.rules; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            # Skip comments/blank lines
            case "$line" in
                \#*|"") continue ;;
            esac
            # Must be an always,exit rule watching the same path with exec perm
            if echo "$line" | grep -Eq -- '-a[[:space:]]+always,exit|-a[[:space:]]+exit,always'; then
                if echo "$line" | grep -Eq -- "-F[[:space:]]+path=${AUDIT_BIN_PATH}([[:space:]]|$)"; then
                    if echo "$line" | grep -Eq -- '-F[[:space:]]+perm=[rwa]*x[rwa]*'; then
                        if echo "$line" | grep -Eq -- '-F[[:space:]]+auid>=1000'; then
                            if echo "$line" | grep -Eq -- '-F[[:space:]]+auid!=(unset|-1|4294967295)'; then

                            fi
                        fi
                    fi
                fi
            fi
        done < "$f"
    done
    return 1
}

    # Ensure file ends with newline before appending
    if [ -s "${RULES_FILE}" ] && [ "$(tail -c1 "${RULES_FILE}" | wc -l)" -eq 0 ]; then
        echo >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    fi
    echo "${RULE}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    CHANGED=1
fi

chmod 0640 "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true
chown root:root "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true

# Load rules into the running kernel so change takes effect immediately,
# unless audit rules are immutable (-e 2), in which case a reboot is needed.
if [ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]; then

        echo "Audit rules are immutable; new rule will take effect after reboot." >&2

                echo "Warning: failed to load audit rules; they will apply on next reboot." >&2
        fi
    fi
    echo "Remediation applied: audit rule for ${AUDIT_BIN_PATH} added."
else
    echo "Already compliant: audit rule for ${AUDIT_BIN_PATH} present."
fi

    echo "${RULE}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    CHANGED=1
fi

chmod 0640 "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true
chown root:root "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true

# Load rules into the running kernel so change takes effect immediately,
# unless audit rules are immutable (-e 2), in which case a reboot is needed.
if [ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]; then
    if auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'enabled 2'; then
        echo "Audit rules are immutable; new rule will take effect after reboot." >&2
    else
        if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
                echo "Warning: failed to load audit rules; they will apply on next reboot." >&2
        else
            auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
                echo "Warning: failed to load audit rules; they will apply on next reboot." >&2
        fi
    fi
    echo "Remediation applied: audit rule for ${AUDIT_BIN_PATH} added."
else
    echo "Already compliant: audit rule for ${AUDIT_BIN_PATH} present."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -u

# Remediation: prevent users from changing the GNOME screensaver lock-delay
# setting by placing a dconf lock in the system database and rebuilding it.

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
LOCKS_DIR="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/locks"
LOCK_FILE="${LOCKS_DIR}/00-security-settings-lock"
LOCK_SETTING="/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay"

# Only applicable when GNOME/dconf is installed
if ! rpm -q dconf >/dev/null 2>&1 && ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "dconf is not installed; remediation is not applicable." >&2
    exit 0
fi

# Ensure the 'local' dconf database is included in the system profile
PROFILE_FILE="/etc/dconf/profile/user"
mkdir -p /etc/dconf/profile
if [ ! -f "${PROFILE_FILE}" ] || ! grep -q '^system-db:local$' "${PROFILE_FILE}"; then
    if [ ! -f "${PROFILE_FILE}" ] || ! grep -q '^user-db:user$' "${PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        printf 'user-db:user\n' >> "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
    if ! grep -q '^system-db:local$' "${PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        printf 'system-db:local\n' >> "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# Ensure the locks directory exists
mkdir -p "${LOCKS_DIR}"

# Check whether the lock already exists anywhere in the locks directory
if ! grep -Rqs "^${LOCK_SETTING}\$" "${LOCKS_DIR}/"; then
    printf '%s\n' "${LOCK_SETTING}" >> "${LOCK_FILE}"
fi

# Enforce sane ownership and permissions on the lock file
chown root:root "${LOCK_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true
chmod 0644 "${LOCK_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true

# Rebuild the dconf databases so the lock takes effect for active sessions
dconf update

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

VAR_VALUE="4"
CONF_FILE="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
CONF_DIR="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"

if [ ! -f "$CONF_FILE" ]; then
    touch "$CONF_FILE"
    chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"
fi

# Remove any conflicting definitions in drop-in directory files
if [ -d "$CONF_DIR" ]; then
    for f in "$CONF_DIR"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -Pq '^\s*minclass\b' "$f"; then
            sed -ri 's/^\s*(minclass\b.*)$/# \1/' "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Ensure exactly one active, correct minclass setting in the main config
if grep -Pq '^\s*minclass\s*=' "$CONF_FILE"; then
    sed -ri "s/^\s*minclass\s*=.*$/minclass = ${VAR_VALUE}/" "$CONF_FILE"
else
    if [ -s "$CONF_FILE" ] && [ "$(tail -c 1 "$CONF_FILE" | wc -l)" -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "" >> "$CONF_FILE"
    fi
    echo "minclass = ${VAR_VALUE}" >> "$CONF_FILE"
fi

# Comment out any commented-duplicate lines left uncommented besides the first active one
active_count="$(grep -Pc '^\s*minclass\s*=' "$CONF_FILE" || true)"
if [ "$active_count" -gt 1 ]; then
    awk -v val="$VAR_VALUE" '
        BEGIN { seen = 0 }
        /^[[:space:]]*minclass[[:space:]]*=/ {
            if (seen == 0) { print "minclass = " val; seen = 1 }
            else { print "# " $0 }
            next
        }
        { print }
    ' "$CONF_FILE" > "${CONF_FILE}.tmp"
    cat "${CONF_FILE}.tmp" > "$CONF_FILE"
    rm -f "${CONF_FILE}.tmp"
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -eu

RULES_FILE="/etc/usbguard/rules.conf"

# Ensure usbguard is installed
if ! command -v usbguard >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf -y install usbguard
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum -y install usbguard
    else
        echo "ERROR: Cannot install usbguard: no package manager found." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Ensure the configuration directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/usbguard

# Only generate a policy if the current rules file is missing or empty,
# so that an existing non-empty policy is not overwritten (idempotent).
if [ ! -s "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
    usbguard generate-policy > /tmp/usbguard_rules.conf
    install -m 0600 -o root -g root /tmp/usbguard_rules.conf "$RULES_FILE"
    rm -f /tmp/usbguard_rules.conf

    # Restart the daemon so the new policy takes effect if it is running
    if systemctl is-active --quiet usbguard.service; then
        systemctl restart usbguard.service
    fi
fi

# Ensure correct ownership/permissions on the rules file
chown root:root "$RULES_FILE"
chmod 0600 "$RULES_FILE"

# Ensure the service is enabled (persists across reboots) and started
if ! systemctl is-enabled --quiet usbguard.service 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl enable usbguard.service
fi

if ! systemctl is-active --quiet usbguard.service; then
    systemctl start usbguard.service
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Must run as root
if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

CRON_FILE="/etc/cron.d/aide"
CRON_ENTRY='0 5 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /usr/bin/mail -s "$HOSTNAME - Daily AIDE integrity check run" root@localhost'

# --- Ensure AIDE is installed ---
if ! rpm -q aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum -y install aide
fi

# --- Ensure a mail client is installed (mailx provides /usr/bin/mail) ---
if ! command -v mail >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum -y install mailx || yum -y install s-nail
fi

# --- Ensure the AIDE database has been initialized ---
if [ ! -e /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz ]; then
    /usr/sbin/aide --init
    if [ -e /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz ]; then
        cp -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
    fi
fi

# --- Check whether an AIDE check-with-mail job already exists anywhere ---
already_configured=0
search_targets=()
[ -f /etc/crontab ] && search_targets+=(/etc/crontab)
[ -d /etc/cron.d ] && search_targets+=(/etc/cron.d)
[ -d /etc/cron.daily ] && search_targets+=(/etc/cron.daily)
[ -f /var/spool/cron/root ] && search_targets+=(/var/spool/cron/root)

if [ "${#search_targets[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
    if grep -Ersq 'aide[[:space:]]+(--check|-C).*\|.*mail' "${search_targets[@]}" 2>/dev/null; then
        already_configured=1
    fi
fi

# --- Create/repair the cron job if not properly configured ---
if [ "$already_configured" -eq 0 ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$CRON_ENTRY" > "$CRON_FILE"
fi

# --- Ensure our managed cron file (if present) has correct ownership/permissions ---
if [ -f "$CRON_FILE" ]; then
    chown root:root "$CRON_FILE"
    chmod 0644 "$CRON_FILE"
    # Restore SELinux context if tooling is available
    if command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        restorecon "$CRON_FILE" || true
    fi
fi

# --- Ensure cron daemon is enabled and running so the job persists across reboots ---
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable crond >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl start crond >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

# Remediation: Ensure account lockouts due to excessive failed login

#

#
# Remediation: Ensure account lockouts due to excessive failed login
# attempts are logged (pam_faillock "audit" option) on RHEL 8.
# Idempotent; changes persist across reboots (config-file based).
#

#
# 1. Ensure /etc/security/faillock.conf exists and has "audit" enabled.
#
if [[ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]]; then
    touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

if grep -Eq '^\s*audit\b' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    : # already enabled, nothing to do
elif grep -Eq '^\s*#\s*audit\b' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    # Uncomment the existing commented-out directive
    sed -ri 's/^\s*#\s*audit\b.*/audit/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
else

fi

#

#    audit setting takes effect.
#
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1 && authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # System is managed by authselect: enable the faillock feature.
    if ! authselect current | grep -q 'with-faillock'; then
        authselect enable-feature with-faillock >/dev/null
    fi
    authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
    # Non-authselect system: edit PAM files directly.

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -u

UID_MIN=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN/{print $2; exit}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null)
UID_MIN=${UID_MIN:-1000}

while IFS=: read -r username _ uid gid _ home shell; do
    # Skip non-interactive accounts
    case "$shell" in
        */nologin|*/false|"") continue ;;
    esac

    # Only local interactive users (UID >= UID_MIN, excluding nfsnobody)
    if [ "$uid" -lt "$UID_MIN" ] || [ "$username" = "nfsnobody" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Skip if home directory does not exist or is root
    if [ -z "$home" ] || [ "$home" = "/" ] || [ ! -d "$home" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Primary group name
    primary_group=$(getent group "$gid" | cut -d: -f1)
    if [ -z "$primary_group" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # All GIDs the user is a member of
    user_gids=$(id -G "$username" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -z "$user_gids" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Fix files/directories whose group is not one of the user's groups
    find "$home" -xdev \( -type f -o -type d -o -type l \) -print0 2>/dev/null |
    while IFS= read -r -d '' item; do
        item_gid=$(stat -c '%g' "$item" 2>/dev/null) || continue
        match=0
        for g in $user_gids; do
            if [ "$item_gid" -eq "$g" ]; then
                match=1
                break
            fi
        done
        if [ "$match" -eq 0 ]; then
            chgrp -h "$primary_group" "$item"
        fi
    done

done < <(getent passwd)

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -u

SYSCTL_PARAMS=("net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding" "net.ipv6.conf.default.forwarding")
SYSCTL_VALUE="0"
PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-ipv6-forwarding.conf"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Skip remediation gracefully if IPv6 is not present on the system
if [ ! -d /proc/sys/net/ipv6 ]; then
    echo "IPv6 is disabled or not available on this system; nothing to do."
    exit 0
fi

# --- Apply runtime settings ---
for param in "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[@]}"; do
    current="$(sysctl -n "$param" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    if [ "$current" != "$SYSCTL_VALUE" ]; then
        sysctl -w "${param}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null
        echo "Runtime: set ${param}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}"
    else
        echo "Runtime: ${param} already set to ${SYSCTL_VALUE}"
    fi
done

# Also disable forwarding on every existing interface at runtime
for iface_dir in /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/*/; do
    fwd_file="${iface_dir}forwarding"
    if [ -w "$fwd_file" ]; then
        if [ "$(cat "$fwd_file")" != "$SYSCTL_VALUE" ]; then
            echo "$SYSCTL_VALUE" > "$fwd_file"
        fi
    fi
done

# --- Remove conflicting persistent entries from other config files ---
CONFIG_LOCATIONS=(/etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf)

for param in "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[@]}"; do

            case "$conf" in

                    # Do not edit vendor/runtime files; they are overridden by /etc
                    ;;
                *)
                    sed -ri "s/^\s*(${param_regex}\s*=.*)$/# \1/" "$conf"
                    echo "Commented out conflicting entry for ${param} in ${conf}"
                    ;;
            esac
        fi
    done
done

# --- Write persistent configuration (idempotent) ---
DESIRED_CONTENT="$(printf '%s = %s\n' "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[0]}" "$SYSCTL_VALUE"; printf '%s = %s\n' "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[1]}" "$SYSCTL_VALUE")"

SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
PARAM="GSSAPIAuthentication"
VALUE="no"

    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
    for f in "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -Eqi "^\s*${PARAM}\b" "$f"; then
            sed -ri "/^\s*${PARAM}\b/Id" "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

if grep -Eqi "^\s*${PARAM}\b" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    # Replace all existing (possibly commented-out duplicates left intact) active lines
    sed -ri "s/^\s*${PARAM}\b.*/${PARAM} ${VALUE}/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    # Insert before the first Match block if one exists, otherwise append
    if grep -Eqi "^\s*Match\b" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        sed -ri "0,/^\s*Match\b/s//${PARAM} ${VALUE}\n&/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    else
        printf '\n%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

occurrences=$(grep -Eci "^\s*${PARAM}\b" "$SSHD_CONFIG" || true)

        BEGIN { IGNORECASE = 1; seen = 0 }
        $0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*"param"([[:space:]]|$)" {
            if (seen == 0) { print param, value; seen = 1 }
            next
        }
        { print }
    ' "$SSHD_CONFIG" > "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    cat "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp" > "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    rm -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
fi

# Restore correct ownership/permissions and SELinux context
chown root:root "$SSHD_CONFIG"
chmod 0600 "$SSHD_CONFIG"
if command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    restorecon -F "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t; then
        echo "Error: sshd configuration validation failed." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd 2>/dev/null; then

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

ARG_NAME="page_poison"
ARG_VALUE="1"
ARG="${ARG_NAME}=${ARG_VALUE}"
GRUB_DEFAULT="/etc/default/grub"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Ensure the argument is present on all currently installed
#    kernels (grubby updates BLS entries / kernelopts on RHEL 8).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Remove any conflicting/incorrect value first, then add the correct one.
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --remove-args="${ARG_NAME}" 2>/dev/null || true
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="${ARG}"
else
    echo "grubby not found; cannot update installed kernel entries." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Ensure the argument persists for future kernels via
#    GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX in /etc/default/grub.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
if [ ! -f "${GRUB_DEFAULT}" ]; then
    touch "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
fi

if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"; then
    # Strip any existing page_poison=<anything> tokens
    sed -i -E "s/(^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"[^\"]*)\b${ARG_NAME}=[^ \"]*/\1/g" "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
    # Collapse duplicate spaces inside the quoted value
    sed -i -E 's/(^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=")[[:space:]]*/\1/; s/[[:space:]]+"/"/; s/[[:space:]]{2,}/ /g' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
    # Append the desired argument if not already present
    if ! grep -qE "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"([^\"]*[[:space:]])?${ARG}([[:space:]][^\"]*)?\"" "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"; then
        sed -i -E "s/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"(.*)\"/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"\1 ${ARG}\"/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
        sed -i -E 's/(^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=")[[:space:]]+/\1/' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
    fi
else
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${ARG}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Also update the shared kernelopts variable in the GRUB
#    environment block if it is in use (pre-RHEL 8.5 BLS setups),
#    so newly installed kernels inherit the option.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
if command -v grub2-editenv >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    existing_kernelopts="$(grub2-editenv - list 2>/dev/null | grep '^kernelopts=' || true)"
    if [ -n "${existing_kernelopts}" ]; then
        opts="${existing_kernelopts#kernelopts=}"
        # Remove any existing page_poison tokens
        cleaned="$(echo "${opts}" | sed -E "s/\b${ARG_NAME}=[^ ]*//g" | tr -s ' ' | sed -E 's/^ //; s/ $//')"
        new_opts="${cleaned} ${ARG}"
        new_opts="$(echo "${new_opts}" | tr -s ' ' | sed -E 's/^ //; s/ $//')"
        if [ "${opts}" != "${new_opts}" ]; then
            grub2-editenv - set "kernelopts=${new_opts}"
        fi
    fi
fi

exit 0

    exit 1

if grep -Eq "^[^#]\S*\s+${MOUNT_POINT}\s+" "$FSTAB" || grep -Eq "^\S+\s+${MOUNT_POINT}\s+" "$FSTAB"; then
    current_line="$(grep -E "^\s*[^#]\S*\s+${MOUNT_POINT}\s+" "$FSTAB" | head -n1 || true)"
    if [[ -n "$current_line" ]]; then
        current_opts="$(echo "$current_line" | awk '{print $4}')"
        if echo "$current_opts" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -qx "$REQUIRED_OPT"; then
            echo "INFO: ${FSTAB} entry for ${MOUNT_POINT} already contains ${REQUIRED_OPT}."
        else
            new_opts="${current_opts},${REQUIRED_OPT}"
            awk -v mp="$MOUNT_POINT" -v newopts="$new_opts" '
                $0 !~ /^\s*#/ && $2 == mp && !done {
                    $4 = newopts
                    done = 1
                    print $1"\t"$2"\t"$3"\t"$4"\t"$5"\t"$6
                    next
                }
                { print }
            ' "$FSTAB" > "${FSTAB}.tmp" && mv "${FSTAB}.tmp" "$FSTAB"

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

GDM_CONF="/etc/gdm/custom.conf"

# Only remediate if GDM is installed on the system
if ! rpm -q gdm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "GDM is not installed; no remediation required."
    exit 0
fi

# Ensure the configuration file and its directory exist
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$GDM_CONF")"
touch "$GDM_CONF"

# Ensure the [daemon] section exists
if ! grep -qi '^\s*\[daemon\]' "$GDM_CONF"; then
    printf '\n[daemon]\n' >> "$GDM_CONF"
fi

set_daemon_key() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"

    if grep -qiE "^\s*${key}\s*=" "$GDM_CONF"; then
        # Key exists somewhere in the file; normalize it to the desired value
        sed -ri "s/^\s*${key}\s*=.*/${key}=${value}/I" "$GDM_CONF"
    else
        # Insert the key immediately after the [daemon] section header
        sed -ri "0,/^\s*\[daemon\]\s*$/s//[daemon]\n${key}=${value}/I" "$GDM_CONF"
    fi
}

# Disable automatic login (no user logged in without credentials)
set_daemon_key "AutomaticLoginEnable" "false"

# Also disable timed login, which would log a user in after a delay
set_daemon_key "TimedLoginEnable" "false"

# Secure sane permissions on the config file
chown root:root "$GDM_CONF"
chmod 0644 "$GDM_CONF"

echo "Automatic graphical login has been disabled in ${GDM_CONF}."
exit 0

set -euo pipefail

CONF="/etc/fapolicyd/fapolicyd.conf"
RULES_DIR="/etc/fapolicyd/rules.d"
LEGACY_RULES="/etc/fapolicyd/fapolicyd.rules"
DENY_RULE="deny_audit perm=any all : all"
CHANGED=0

# 1. Install fapolicyd if not already present
if ! rpm -q fapolicyd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum -y install fapolicyd
    CHANGED=1
fi

# 2. Ensure enforcing mode (permissive = 0) in fapolicyd.conf
if [ -f "$CONF" ]; then
    if grep -Eq '^\s*permissive\s*=' "$CONF"; then
        if ! grep -Eq '^\s*permissive\s*=\s*0\s*$' "$CONF"; then
            sed -ri 's/^\s*permissive\s*=.*/permissive = 0/' "$CONF"
            CHANGED=1
        fi
    else
        echo "permissive = 0" >> "$CONF"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
fi

# 3. Ensure the final rule is an explicit deny-all (default-deny posture)
if [ -d "$RULES_DIR" ] && command -v fagenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    DENY_FILE="$RULES_DIR/99-deny-everything.rules"
    if [ ! -f "$DENY_FILE" ] || ! grep -Fxq "$DENY_RULE" "$DENY_FILE"; then
        printf '%s\n' "$DENY_RULE" > "$DENY_FILE"
        chmod 0644 "$DENY_FILE"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
    # Verify no later rules file overrides the deny; regenerate compiled rules
    fagenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || fagenrules >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
elif [ -f "$LEGACY_RULES" ]; then
    LAST_RULE="$(grep -Ev '^\s*(#|$)' "$LEGACY_RULES" | tail -n 1 || true)"
    if [ "$LAST_RULE" != "$DENY_RULE" ]; then
        printf '%s\n' "$DENY_RULE" >> "$LEGACY_RULES"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
else
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LEGACY_RULES")"
    printf '%s\n' "$DENY_RULE" > "$LEGACY_RULES"
    chmod 0644 "$LEGACY_RULES"
    CHANGED=1
fi

# 4. Enable and start fapolicyd so the policy persists across reboots
if ! systemctl is-enabled fapolicyd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable fapolicyd
    CHANGED=1
fi

if ! systemctl is-active fapolicyd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl start fapolicyd
elif [ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]; then
    systemctl restart fapolicyd
fi

# 5. Verify final state
if systemctl is-active fapolicyd >/dev/null 2>&1 && systemctl is-enabled fapolicyd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Remediation complete: fapolicyd is installed, enforcing (permissive=0), default-deny rule in place, enabled and running."
    exit 0
else
    echo "Remediation FAILED: fapolicyd is not active/enabled." >&2
    exit 1

    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "$CONF" ]]; then
    touch "$CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$CONF"
fi

# Ensure a [Login] section exists
if ! grep -qE '^\s*\[Login\]' "$CONF"; then
    printf '\n[Login]\n' >> "$CONF"
fi

    echo "OK: ${PARAM}=${VALUE} already configured in ${CONF}."
else
    if grep -qE "^\s*#?\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$CONF"; then
        # Replace any existing (commented or wrong-value) occurrence(s)
        sed -ri "s/^\s*#?\s*${PARAM}\s*=.*/${PARAM}=${VALUE}/" "$CONF"
    else
        # Insert directly after the [Login] section header
        sed -ri "0,/^\s*\[Login\]/s//[Login]\n${PARAM}=${VALUE}/" "$CONF"
    fi
    changed=1
    echo "FIXED: Set ${PARAM}=${VALUE} in ${CONF}."
fi

# Deduplicate: keep only the first active occurrence if multiple exist
count=$(grep -cE "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$CONF" || true)
if [[ "$count" -gt 1 ]]; then
    awk -v p="$PARAM" '
        $0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*"p"[[:space:]]*=" {
            if (seen) next
            seen = 1
        }
        { print }
    ' "$CONF" > "${CONF}.tmp"
    cat "${CONF}.tmp" > "$CONF"
    rm -f "${CONF}.tmp"
    changed=1
    echo "FIXED: Removed duplicate ${PARAM} entries from ${CONF}."
fi

# Apply the setting to the running login manager if a change was made
if [[ "$changed" -eq 1 ]]; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet systemd-logind; then
        systemctl restart systemd-logind || \
            echo "WARNING: Could not restart systemd-logind; setting applies on next boot." >&2
    fi
    echo "Remediation complete: idle sessions will be terminated after ${VALUE} seconds."
else
    echo "No changes required; system already compliant."
fi

        printf '%s = %s\n' "$param" "$value" >> "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"
    fi
done

chmod 0644 "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"
chown root:root "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"

# Apply the settings at runtime immediately
for param in "${!PARAMS[@]}"; do
    sysctl -w "${param}=${PARAMS[$param]}" >/dev/null
done

# Flush the routing cache so the change takes effect immediately
sysctl -w net.ipv4.route.flush=1 >/dev/null

# Verify runtime state
rc=0
for param in "${!PARAMS[@]}"; do
    current="$(sysctl -n "$param")"
    if [[ "$current" != "${PARAMS[$param]}" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: ${param} is ${current}, expected ${PARAMS[$param]}" >&2
        rc=1
    fi
done

if [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "Remediation complete: ICMP redirect sending disabled on all IPv4 interfaces (runtime and persistent)."
fi

exit "$rc"

    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

    local file="$1"
    local arch="$2"
    local rule="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"

    # Rule file consumed by auditctl-based loading
    ensure_rule "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" "$arch"

elif command -v auditctl

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_PARAM="net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_source_route"
SYSCTL_VALUE="0"
PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-ipv6-source-route.conf"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -e "/proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/default/accept_source_route" ]; then
    echo "INFO: IPv6 appears to be disabled on this system; nothing to do."
    exit 0
fi

# --- Apply the setting at runtime ---
current_value="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}")"
if [ "${current_value}" != "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" ]; then
    sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}"
    echo "INFO: Runtime value of ${SYSCTL_PARAM} set to ${SYSCTL_VALUE}."
else
    echo "INFO: Runtime value of ${SYSCTL_PARAM} already ${SYSCTL_VALUE}."
fi

# --- Remove or correct conflicting persistent entries ---
# Search all files that sysctl reads at boot and fix any conflicting settings.
config_files=()
[ -f /etc/sysctl.conf ] && config_files+=("/etc/sysctl.conf")
for dir in /etc/sysctl.d /run/sysctl.d /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d /usr/lib/sysctl.d; do
    if [ -d "${dir}" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
            config_files+=("${f}")
        done < <(find "${dir}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
    fi
done

for f in "${config_files[@]}"; do
    [ "${f}" = "${PERSIST_FILE}" ] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=" "${f}"; then
        if grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=\s*${SYSCTL_VALUE}\s*$" "${f}" && \
           ! grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=\s*[^0[:space:]]" "${f}"; then
            continue
        fi
        sed -ri "s|^\s*(${SYSCTL_PARAM})\s*=.*$|\1 = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}|" "${f}"
        echo "INFO: Corrected conflicting entry in ${f}."
    fi
done

# --- Ensure persistent configuration ---
if [ -f "${PERSIST_FILE}" ] && grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=\s*${SYSCTL_VALUE}\s*$" "${PERSIST_FILE}"; then
    echo "INFO: Persistent setting already present in ${PERSIST_FILE}."
else
    printf '%s = %s\n' "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" > "${PERSIST_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${PERSIST_FILE}"
    echo "INFO: Persistent setting written to ${PERSIST_FILE}."
fi

# --- Reload sysctl settings to confirm ---
sysctl --system > /dev/null 2>&1 || true

final_value="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}")"
if [ "${final_value}" = "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" ]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${SYSCTL_PARAM} is set to ${SYSCTL_VALUE} and persisted."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: ${SYSCTL_PARAM} is '${final_value}', expected '${SYSCTL_VALUE}'." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Remediation: Audit all uses of the delete_module syscall (kernel module unloading)
# on RHEL 8 for both 32-bit and 64-bit architectures, keyed as "modules".

set -u

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/modules.rules"

# Ensure audit package/daemon is present
if ! command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf -y install audit >/dev/null 2>&1
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum -y install audit >/dev/null 2>&1
    fi
fi

mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"
chmod 0750 "$RULES_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true

# Desired rules (both architectures, non-system users, keyed "modules")
declare -a DESIRED_RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S delete_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k modules"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S delete_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k modules"
)

# Function: check whether an equivalent rule already exists in rules.d
rule_exists() {
    local arch="$1"
    grep -rEhs '^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)' "$RULES_DIR"/*.rules 2>/dev/null | \
    grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+arch=${arch}\b" | \
    grep -E -- "(-S[[:space:]]+[^ ]*\bdelete_module\b)" | \
    grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+auid>=1000\b" | \
    grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+auid!=(unset|4294967295|-1)\b" | \
    grep -qE -- "(-k[[:space:]]+modules\b|-F[[:space:]]+key=modules\b)"
}

CHANGED=0

for rule in "${DESIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    arch=$(echo "$rule" | grep -oE 'arch=b(32|64)' | cut -d= -f2)
    if ! rule_exists "$arch"; then
        # Ensure file exists before appending
        touch "$RULES_FILE"
        chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
        # Avoid duplicate exact lines in our own file
        if ! grep -qxF -- "$rule" "$RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "$rule" >> "$RULES_FILE"
        fi
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done

# Enable and start auditd so rules persist and are active across reboots
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    if ! systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        systemctl start auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || service auditd start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

# Load the rules into the running kernel if anything changed (or ensure loaded)
if [ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    else
        auditctl -R "$RULES_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

# Verify active rules contain the delete_module audit; attempt one reload if not
if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'delete_module'; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

if auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'delete_module'; then
    echo "Audit rules for kernel module unloading (delete_module) are active and persistent."
    exit 0
else
    # Rules are persisted on disk even if immutable mode (-e 2) blocks live loading
    if grep -rqs 'delete_module' "$RULES_DIR"; then
        echo "Audit rules persisted to disk; a reboot may be required to activate (audit may be in immutable mode)."
        exit 0
    fi
    echo "Failed to configure audit rules for delete_module." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -u

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

SERVICE="debug-shell.service"
RC=0

if ! command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: systemctl not found; cannot remediate." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Stop the service if it is currently active
if systemctl is-active --quiet "$SERVICE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Stopping $SERVICE ..."
    if systemctl stop "$SERVICE"; then
        echo "Stopped $SERVICE."
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to stop $SERVICE." >&2
        RC=1
    fi
else
    echo "$SERVICE is not active."
fi

# Disable the service so it does not start at boot
enabled_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "$SERVICE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ "$enabled_state" = "enabled" ] || [ "$enabled_state" = "enabled-runtime" ]; then
    echo "Disabling $SERVICE ..."
    if systemctl disable "$SERVICE"; then
        echo "Disabled $SERVICE."
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to disable $SERVICE." >&2
        RC=1
    fi
else
    echo "$SERVICE is not enabled (state: ${enabled_state:-unknown})."
fi

# Mask the service so it cannot be started manually or as a dependency
if [ "$(systemctl is-enabled "$SERVICE" 2>/dev/null || true)" = "masked" ]; then
    echo "$SERVICE is already masked."
else
    echo "Masking $SERVICE ..."
    if systemctl mask "$SERVICE"; then
        echo "Masked $SERVICE."
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to mask $SERVICE." >&2
        RC=1
    fi
fi

systemctl daemon-reload

# Verify final state
final_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "$SERVICE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ "$final_state" = "masked" ] && ! systemctl is-active --quiet "$SERVICE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "SUCCESS: $SERVICE is stopped and masked; remediation complete."
else
    echo "ERROR: Verification failed. Current state: ${final_state:-unknown}" >&2
    RC=1
fi

exit "$RC"

if [ "$final_value" = "$VALUE" ]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: audit events will be tagged with the FQDN node identifier."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to enforce ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} in ${AUDITD_CONF}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
PARAM="StrictModes"
VALUE="yes"

if [ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $SSHD_CONFIG not found. Is openssh-server installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the config once per run (timestamped, does not affect idempotency of the setting)
cp -p "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Remove any existing occurrences of the directive (active or commented, any case, any value)
# from the main config file, then insert the correct directive.
if grep -Eqi "^\s*${PARAM}\b" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    # Replace the first active occurrence with the desired value
    sed -ri "0,/^\s*${PARAM}\s+.*/s//${PARAM} ${VALUE}/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    # Comment out any additional active occurrences to avoid conflicting duplicates
    sed -ri "s/^\s*(${PARAM}\s+.*)/${PARAM} ${VALUE}/I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    # Deduplicate: keep only the first occurrence of the directive
    awk -v param="$PARAM" '
        tolower($1) == tolower(param) {
            if (seen) next
            seen = 1
        }
        { print }
    ' "$SSHD_CONFIG" > "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    mv "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    printf '\n%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

# Also ensure no conflicting directive exists in drop-in include files (if present)
if [ -d /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d ]; then
    for f in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -Eqi "^\s*${PARAM}\s+" "$f"; then
            sed -ri "s/^\s*(${PARAM}\s+.*)/${PARAM} ${VALUE}/I" "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Restore correct ownership, permissions, and SELinux context on the config
chown root:root "$SSHD_CONFIG"
chmod 600 "$SSHD_CONFIG"
command -v restorecon >/dev/null 2>&1 && restorecon -F "$SSHD_CONFIG" || true

# Validate configuration before applying
if ! sshd -t; then
    echo "ERROR: sshd configuration validation failed; not restarting sshd." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure sshd is enabled at boot (persistence) and reload the running service
systemctl enable sshd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
    systemctl restart sshd
fi

# Verify the effective runtime setting
if sshd -T 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "^strictmodes yes$"; then
    echo "OK: ${PARAM} is set to ${VALUE} and enforced."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: ${PARAM} is not effectively set to ${VALUE}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

fi

    grep -rhEq -- \

}

    if ! rule_exists "$arch"; then
        echo "-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S unlink -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=delete" >> "$RULES_FILE"

MAX_PERM_DEC=$((8#$MAX_PERM_OCT))

            [ -z "$current_perms" ] && current_perms=""

            local missing=false
            local new_perms="$current_perms"
            local i

                local bit="${required_access_bits:$i:1}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -u

AUDIT_PATH="/etc/passwd"
REQUIRED_PERMS="wa"
KEY="audit_rules_usergroup_modification"

function fix_audit_watch_rule {
    local tool="$1"
    local path="$2"
    local required_access_bits="$3"
    local key="$4"

    local files_to_inspect=()
    local audit_file=""

    if [ "$tool" == "auditctl" ]; then
        audit_file="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
        files_to_inspect+=("$audit_file")
    elif [ "$tool" == "augenrules" ]; then
        audit_file="/etc/audit/rules.d/${key}.rules"
        readarray -t matches < <(grep -HP "[\s]*-w[\s]+$path" /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f1 | sort -u)
        for match in "${matches[@]}"; do
            files_to_inspect+=("$match")
        done
        if [ ${#files_to_inspect[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
            files_to_inspect+=("$audit_file")
        fi
    else
        return 1
    fi

    for inspected_file in "${files_to_inspect[@]}"; do
        if [ ! -e "$inspected_file" ]; then
            continue
        fi

        readarray -t existing_rules < <(sed -e "/^[^#].*-w[[:space:]]\+$path/!d" "$inspected_file")

        for rule in "${existing_rules[@]}"; do
            # Extract current permissions from the rule
            local current_perms
            current_perms=$(echo "$rule" | grep -oP '(?<=-p[\s])+\S+' | head -n1)
            [ -z "$current_perms" ] && current_perms=""

            local missing=false
            local new_perms="$current_perms"
            local i
            for (( i=0; i<${#required_access_bits}; i++ )); do
                local bit="${required_access_bits:$i:1}"
                if [[ "$new_perms" != *"$bit"* ]]; then
                    new_perms="${new_perms}${bit}"
                    missing=true
                fi
            done

            if $missing; then
                local escaped_rule
                escaped_rule=$(printf '%s' "$rule" | sed 's/[][\\/.^$*]/\\&/g')
                local new_rule
                if [ -n "$current_perms" ]; then
                    new_rule=$(echo "$rule" | sed "s/-p[[:space:]]\+$current_perms/-p $new_perms/")
                else
                    new_rule="$rule -p $new_perms"
                fi
                sed -i "s/^${escaped_rule}\$/$(printf '%s' "$new_rule" | sed 's/[\\/&]/\\&/g')/" "$inspected_file"
            fi
        done

        if [ ${#existing_rules[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
            # A rule for this path already exists (and has been fixed if needed)
            return 0
        fi
    done

    # No existing rule found; append a new one to the appropriate file
    if [ ! -e "$audit_file" ]; then
        touch "$audit_file"
    fi
    chmod 0640 "$audit_file"
    echo "-w $path -p $required_access_bits -k $key" >> "$audit_file"
    return 0
}

fix_audit_watch_rule "auditctl" "$AUDIT_PATH" "$REQUIRED_PERMS" "$KEY"
fix_audit_watch_rule "augenrules" "$AUDIT_PATH" "$REQUIRED_PERMS" "$KEY"

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Audit all uses of the removexattr syscall on RHEL 8
# Applies rules for both augenrules (/etc/audit/rules.d/) and
# auditctl (/etc/audit/audit.rules) persistence mechanisms.
#
set -u

RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_D_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/perm_mod.rules"
AUDITCTL_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# Determine which architectures to configure (b32 always; b64 on 64-bit kernels)
ARCHS=("b32")
if [ "$(getconf LONG_BIT 2>/dev/null || echo 32)" = "64" ]; then
    ARCHS+=("b64")
fi

# Build the required audit rules
RULES=()
for arch in "${ARCHS[@]}"; do
    RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S removexattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod")
    RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S removexattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod")
done

# Ensure target files exist with correct ownership/permissions
mkdir -p "$RULES_D_DIR"
for f in "$RULES_D_FILE" "$AUDITCTL_FILE"; do
    if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
        touch "$f"
    fi
    chown root:root "$f"
    chmod 0640 "$f"
done

# Append a rule to a file only if an equivalent rule is not already present
ensure_rule() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"
    local arch auid_expr

    arch="$(echo "$rule" | grep -oE 'arch=b(32|64)')"
    if echo "$rule" | grep -q 'auid>=1000'; then
        auid_expr='auid>=1000'
    else
        auid_expr='auid=0([^0-9]|$)'
    fi

    # Match an existing equivalent rule: always,exit action, same arch,
    # removexattr syscall, same auid filter (key name may vary in form).
    if grep -E -- "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+(always,exit|exit,always)[[:space:]]" "$file" 2>/dev/null \
        | grep -E -- "-F[[:space:]]+${arch}\b" \
        | grep -E -- "-S[[:space:]]+[^[:space:]]*\bremovexattr\b" \
        | grep -Eq -- "-F[[:space:]]+${auid_expr}"
    then
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$rule" >> "$file"
}

for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    ensure_rule "$RULES_D_FILE" "$rule"
    ensure_rule "$AUDITCTL_FILE" "$rule"
done

# Load the rules into the running audit daemon (best effort; may be
# deferred to next reboot if rules are immutable, e.g. -e 2 is set)
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "$AUDITCTL_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

if [[ ! -f "$CONF_FILE" ]]; then

            sed -ri "s/^\s*(minlen\s*=\s*).*$/\1${VAR_PASSWORD_MINLEN}/" "$dropin"

        sed -ri "0,/^\s*#\s*minlen\b.*$/s//minlen = ${VAR_PASSWORD_MINLEN}/" "$CONF_FILE"

    if authselect check >/dev/null 2>&1; then

            authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Verify final state

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Audit all uses of the fsetxattr syscall on RHEL 8
# - Covers b32 and b64 architectures
# - Covers unprivileged users (auid>=1000, auid!=unset) and root (auid=0)
# - Tags events with key "perm_mod"
# - Persistent, idempotent, integrates with augenrules or auditctl loading

set -u

# The audit rules we must ensure exist
RULES=(
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fsetxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fsetxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fsetxattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fsetxattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"
)

# Determine which rule-loading mechanism auditd uses
uses_augenrules() {
    local svc
    for svc in /usr/lib/systemd/system/auditd.service /etc/systemd/system/auditd.service; do
        if [ -f "$svc" ]; then
            if grep -qE '^\s*ExecStartPost=.*augenrules' "$svc"; then
                return 0
            fi
            if grep -qE '^\s*ExecStartPost=.*auditctl' "$svc"; then
                return 1
            fi
        fi
    done
    # Default on RHEL 8 is augenrules
    return 0
}

# Normalize a rule line for comparison (collapse whitespace, unify key syntax)
normalize() {
    echo "$1" | sed -e 's/[[:space:]]\+/ /g' \
                    -e 's/^ //' -e 's/ $//' \
                    -e 's/-k \([^ ]\+\)/-F key=\1/'
}

# Check whether an equivalent rule already exists in a file
rule_in_file() {
    local rule="$1" file="$2" norm_rule line
    [ -f "$file" ] || return 1
    norm_rule="$(normalize "$rule")"
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        case "$line" in
            \#*|"") continue ;;
        esac
        if [ "$(normalize "$line")" = "$norm_rule" ]; then
            return 0
        fi
    done < "$file"
    return 1
}

# Ensure a rule exists in a given file, appending if missing
ensure_rule() {
    local rule="$1" file="$2"
    if ! rule_in_file "$rule" "$file"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

ensure_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        touch "$file"
    fi
    chown root:root "$file" 2>/dev/null || true
    chmod 0640 "$file" 2>/dev/null || true
}

changed=0

if uses_augenrules; then
    RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
    mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"

    for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        # See if an equivalent rule already exists in any rules.d file
        found=0
        for f in "$RULES_DIR"/*.rules; do
            [ -e "$f" ] || continue
            if rule_in_file "$rule" "$f"; then
                found=1
                break
            fi
        done
        if [ "$found" -eq 0 ]; then
            TARGET="$RULES_DIR/audit.rules"
            ensure_file "$TARGET"
            echo "$rule" >> "$TARGET"
            changed=1
        fi
    done

    if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
        augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
else
    TARGET="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
    mkdir -p /etc/audit
    ensure_file "$TARGET"

    for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        if ! rule_in_file "$rule" "$TARGET"; then
            echo "$rule" >> "$TARGET"
            changed=1
        fi
    done

    if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
        auditctl -R "$TARGET" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

# The audit rules we must ensure exist
RULES=(
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fsetxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fsetxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fsetxattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fsetxattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"
)

# Determine which rule-loading mechanism auditd uses
uses_augenrules() {
    local svc
    for svc in /usr/lib/systemd/system/auditd.service /etc/systemd/system/auditd.service; do
        if [ -f "$svc" ]; then
            if grep -qE '^\s*ExecStartPost=.*augenrules' "$svc"; then
                return 0
            fi
            if grep -qE '^\s*ExecStartPost=.*auditctl' "$svc"; then
                return 1
            fi
        fi
    done
    # Default on RHEL 8 is augenrules
    return 0
}

# Normalize a rule line for comparison (collapse whitespace, unify key syntax)
normalize() {
    echo "$1" | sed -e 's/[[:space:]]\+/ /g' \
                    -e 's/^ //' -e 's/ $//' \
                    -e 's/-k \([^ ]\+\)/-F key=\1/'
}

# Check whether an equivalent rule already exists in a file
rule_in_file() {
    local rule="$1" file="$2" norm_rule line
    [ -f "$file" ] || return 1
    norm_rule="$(normalize "$rule")"
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        case "$line" in
            \#*|"") continue ;;
        esac
        if [ "$(normalize "$line")" = "$norm_rule" ]; then
            return 0
        fi
    done < "$file"
    return 1
}

# Ensure a rule exists in a given file, appending if missing
ensure_rule() {
    local rule="$1" file="$2"
    if ! rule_in_file "$rule" "$file"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

ensure_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        touch "$file"
    fi
    chown root:root "$file" 2>/dev/null || true
    chmod 0640 "$file" 2>/dev/null || true
}

changed=0

if uses_augenrules; then
    RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
    mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"

    for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        # See if an equivalent rule already exists in any rules.d file
        found=0
        for f in "$RULES_DIR"/*.rules; do
            [ -e "$f" ] || continue
            if rule_in_file "$rule" "$f"; then
                found=1
                break
            fi
        done
        if [ "$found" -eq 0 ]; then
            TARGET="$RULES_DIR/audit.rules"
            ensure_file "$TARGET"
            echo "$rule" >> "$TARGET"
            changed=1
        fi
    done

    if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
        augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
else
    TARGET="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
    mkdir -p /etc/audit
    ensure_file "$TARGET"

    for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        if ! rule_in_file "$rule" "$TARGET"; then
            echo "$rule" >> "$TARGET"
            changed=1
        fi
    done

    if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
        auditctl -R "$TARGET" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

exit 0
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Remediation: ensure non-hidden files/dirs in local interactive users' home
# directories are mode 0750 or stricter.

set -u

# Determine minimum UID for interactive users (default 1000 on RHEL 8)
UID_MIN="$(awk '$1=="UID_MIN"{print $2}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null)"
[ -n "${UID_MIN}" ] || UID_MIN=1000

while IFS=: read -r user _pass uid _gid _gecos home shell; do
    # Only local interactive users
    [ -n "${uid}" ] || continue
    [ "${uid}" -ge "${UID_MIN}" ] || continue

    # Skip non-interactive shells
    case "${shell}" in
        ""|*/nologin|*/false|/sbin/halt|/sbin/shutdown|/bin/sync)
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Home directory must exist and be sane
    [ -n "${home}" ] || continue
    [ "${home}" != "/" ] || continue
    [ -d "${home}" ] || continue

    # Find non-hidden files/directories (prune anything starting with a dot)
    # that have any permission bits beyond 0750:
    #   07027 = setuid/setgid/sticky (7000) + group-write (0020) + other-rwx (0007)
    # Removing only the excess bits makes this idempotent and never loosens perms.
    find "${home}" -mindepth 1 -name '.*' -prune -o \
        \( -type f -o -type d \) -perm /7027 \
        -exec chmod u-s,g-ws,o-rwxt {} + 2>/dev/null

done < /etc/passwd

exit 0

set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

PARAM="user.max_user_namespaces"
VALUE="0"
CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-user-namespaces.conf"

for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [[ -e "$f" ]] || continue
    [[ "$f" == "$CONF_FILE" ]] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$f"; then
        if [[ -w "$f" ]]; then
            sed -ri "s|^\s*(${PARAM})\s*=.*|# \1 setting overridden by ${CONF_FILE}|" "$f"
        fi
    fi
done

desired_line="${PARAM} = ${VALUE}"
if [[ ! -f "$CONF_FILE" ]] || ! grep -Fxq "$desired_line" "$CONF_FILE"; then
    printf '%s\n' "$desired_line" > "$CONF_FILE"
fi
chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"
chown root:root "$CONF_FILE"

current="$(sysctl -n "$PARAM")"
if [[ "$current" != "$VALUE" ]]; then
    sysctl -w "${PARAM}=${VALUE}" > /dev/null
fi

#
# Remediation: Disable acceptance of ICMP redirect messages on all IPv4 interfaces
# Applies the setting at runtime and persists it across reboots.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

# Must be root
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

PARAMS=(
    "net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects"
    "net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects"
)
DESIRED_VALUE="0"
PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-icmp-redirects.conf"
