# 1. Apply the setting at runtime
#
for param in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
    current="$(sysctl -n "${param}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    if [[ "${current}" != "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
        sysctl -w "${param}=${DESIRED_VALUE}" >/dev/null
        echo "Runtime: set ${param} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}"
    else
        echo "Runtime: ${param} already set to ${DESIRED_VALUE}"
    fi
done

# Also apply to all currently existing interfaces immediately
for iface_path in /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/*/accept_redirects; do
    [[ -e "${iface_path}" ]] || continue
    if [[ "$(cat "${iface_path}")" != "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
        echo "${DESIRED_VALUE}" > "${iface_path}"
    fi
done

# Flush the routing cache so any routes learned via redirects are discarded
sysctl -w net.ipv4.route.flush=1 >/dev/null

#
# 2. Remove/override any conflicting persistent entries in sysctl config files
#
CONF_FILES=()
[[ -f /etc/sysctl.conf ]] && CONF_FILES+=("/etc/sysctl.conf")
for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [[ -f "$f" ]] && CONF_FILES+=("$f")
done

for f in "${CONF_FILES[@]}"; do
    # Skip our own persistence file; it is rewritten below
    [[ "$f" == "${PERSIST_FILE}" ]] && continue
    for param in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
        # Comment out any active line setting this parameter to a non-zero value
        if grep -Eq "^\s*${param}\s*=\s*[^0]" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -ri "s|^(\s*${param}\s*=\s*[^0].*)$|# \1  # disabled by ICMP redirect remediation|" "$f"
            echo "Sanitized conflicting entry for ${param} in $f"
        fi
    done
done

#
# 3. Persist the desired settings
#
DESIRED_CONTENT="# Managed by ICMP redirect remediation script - do not edit manually
# Refuse ICMP redirect messages on all IPv4 interfaces (MITM protection)
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
"

if [[ ! -f "${PERSIST_FILE}" ]] || [[ "$(cat "${PERSIST_FILE}")" != "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" ]]; then
    printf '%s' "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" > "${PERSIST_FILE}"
    chown root:root "${PERSIST_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${PERSIST_FILE}"
    echo "Persistence: wrote ${PERSIST_FILE}"
else
    echo "Persistence: ${PERSIST_FILE} already correct"
fi

# Reload sysctl settings from the persistence file to confirm they apply cleanly
sysctl -p "${PERSIST_FILE}" >/dev/null

#
# 4. Verify final state
#
rc=0
for param in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
    val="$(sysctl -n "${param}")"
    if [[ "${val}" == "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
        echo "Verified: ${param} = ${val}"
    else
        echo "ERROR: ${param} is ${val}, expected ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >&2
        rc=1
    fi
done

exit "${rc}"

RULE_B64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1

    yum -y install audit >/dev/null 2>&1 || dnf -y install audit >/dev/null 2>&1
fi

mkdir -p "$(dirname "${RULES_FILE}")"
touch "${RULES_FILE}"
chmod 0640 "${RULES_FILE}"

add_rule_if_missing() {
    local arch="$1"
    local rule="$2"
    local pattern="^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]]+always,exit[[:space:]]+.*-F[[:space:]]+arch=${arch}\b.*(-S[[:space:]]+[^[:space:]]*\bchown\b).*"

        echo "${rule}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    fi
}

add_rule_if_missing "b64" "${RULE_B64}"

if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1
else
    auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules >/dev/null 2>&1
fi

systemctl enable auditd.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

if auditctl -l | grep -q -- "chown"; then
    echo "Audit rules for chown (key=${KEY}) are configured and loaded."
else
    echo "Audit rules written to ${RULES_FILE}; a reboot or auditd restart may be required to load them." >&2
fi

exit 0

    echo "OK: ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} is configured in ${CONF_FILE}."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to configure ${PARAM} in ${CONF_FILE}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#
# Remediation: Limit each user account to a maximum of 10 concurrent
# login sessions on RHEL 8 (per-user maxlogins cap via pam_limits).
#
# This script is idempotent and persists across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

MAXLOGINS_VALUE=10
LIMITS_CONF="/etc/security/limits.conf"
LIMITS_D_DIR="/etc/security/limits.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${LIMITS_D_DIR}/maxlogins.conf"
DESIRED_LINE="* hard maxlogins ${MAXLOGINS_VALUE}"

if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure limits.d directory exists
mkdir -p "${LIMITS_D_DIR}"
chmod 0755 "${LIMITS_D_DIR}"

#
# 1. Sanitize /etc/security/limits.conf:
#    Comment out any active (non-comment) maxlogins entries so they do not
#    conflict with or override the drop-in configuration.
#
if [[ -f "${LIMITS_CONF}" ]]; then
    if grep -Eq '^\s*[^#[:space:]]+\s+(hard|soft|-)\s+maxlogins\s+' "${LIMITS_CONF}"; then
        sed -ri 's/^(\s*[^#[:space:]]+\s+(hard|soft|-)\s+maxlogins\s+.*)$/# \1/' "${LIMITS_CONF}"
    fi
fi

#
# 2. Sanitize other drop-in files in /etc/security/limits.d/:
#    Comment out active maxlogins entries in any file other than our
#    managed drop-in file.
#
for f in "${LIMITS_D_DIR}"/*.conf; do
    [[ -e "$f" ]] || continue
    [[ "$f" == "${DROPIN_FILE}" ]] && continue
    if grep -Eq '^\s*[^#[:space:]]+\s+(hard|soft|-)\s+maxlogins\s+' "$f"; then
        sed -ri 's/^(\s*[^#[:space:]]+\s+(hard|soft|-)\s+maxlogins\s+.*)$/# \1/' "$f"
    fi
done

#
# 3. Enforce the desired setting in the managed drop-in file.
#    Only rewrite the file if its effective content differs (idempotence).
#
current_effective=""
if [[ -f "${DROPIN_FILE}" ]]; then
    current_effective="$(grep -E '^\s*\*\s+hard\s+maxlogins\s+[0-9]+\s*$' "${DROPIN_FILE}" \
        | awk '{print $1, $2, $3, $4}' || true)"
fi

if [[ "${current_effective}" != "${DESIRED_LINE}" ]]; then
    cat > "${DROPIN_FILE}" <<EOF
# Managed by remediation script: limit concurrent login sessions per user.
# Any single account is restricted to at most ${MAXLOGINS_VALUE} simultaneous logins.
${DESIRED_LINE}
EOF
    echo "Remediated: wrote '${DESIRED_LINE}' to ${DROPIN_FILE}"
else
    echo "Compliant: '${DESIRED_LINE}' already configured in ${DROPIN_FILE}"
fi

# Enforce sane ownership/permissions on the drop-in file
chown root:root "${DROPIN_FILE}"
chmod 0644 "${DROPIN_FILE}"

#
# 4. Verify final state.
#
if grep -Eq "^\s*\*\s+hard\s+maxlogins\s+${MAXLOGINS_VALUE}\s*$" "${DROPIN_FILE}"; then
    echo "Verification passed: per-user concurrent session limit is ${MAXLOGINS_VALUE}."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Verification failed; maxlogins limit not correctly configured." >&2
    exit 1
fi

        cp -f "$AIDE_NEW_DB" "$AIDE_DB"

        cp -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db
    else
        echo "ERROR: AIDE initialization did not produce a new database." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Secure permissions on the database

    chown root:root "$AIDE_DB"
    chmod 0600 "$AIDE_DB"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Audit file deletion and rename events on RHEL 8
# Records rename/removal syscalls for all regular users (auid>=1000, auid!=unset)
# Rules persist via /etc/audit/rules.d and are loaded by auditd via augenrules.
# Script is idempotent.

set -euo pipefail

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/30-delete.rules"
AUDIT_KEY="delete"
SYSCALLS="rename,renameat,unlink,unlinkat,rmdir"

mkdir -p "$RULES_DIR"
touch "$RULES_FILE"
chown root:root "$RULES_FILE"
chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"

# Determine applicable architectures
ARCHS=("b32")
if [ "$(getconf LONG_BIT 2>/dev/null || echo 32)" = "64" ]; then
    ARCHS+=("b64")
fi

changed=0

for arch in "${ARCHS[@]}"; do
    rule="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${SYSCALLS} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

    # Check if an equivalent rule already exists in any rules.d file (idempotency).
    # A rule is considered present if a single line contains the arch, all syscalls,
    # the auid filters, and is an always,exit rule.
    found=0
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            # Skip comments/blank lines
            case "$line" in
                \#*|"") continue ;;
            esac
            if echo "$line" | grep -q -- "-a always,exit" \
               && echo "$line" | grep -q -- "arch=${arch}" \
               && echo "$line" | grep -q -- "auid>=1000" \
               && echo "$line" | grep -Eq -- "auid!=(unset|4294967295|-1)"; then
                all_present=1
                for sc in rename renameat unlink unlinkat rmdir; do
                    if ! echo "$line" | grep -qw -- "$sc"; then
                        all_present=0
                        break
                    fi
                done
                if [ "$all_present" -eq 1 ]; then
                    found=1
                    break
                fi
            fi
        done < "$f"
        [ "$found" -eq 1 ] && break
    done < <(find "$RULES_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.rules' -print0)

    if [ "$found" -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$RULES_FILE"
        changed=1
    fi
done

# Ensure auditd is enabled to load rules at startup
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# Load the rules now if anything changed and the system allows it
if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # augenrules --load fails harmlessly if rules are immutable (-e 2); reboot required then
        if ! augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "NOTE: Audit rules written but could not be loaded live (rules may be immutable). A reboot is required." >&2
        fi
    fi
    echo "Audit rules for file deletion/rename events have been configured in ${RULES_FILE}."
else
    echo "Audit rules for file deletion/rename events already present. No changes made."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure rsyslog off-loads (forwards) log records over an
# encrypted TLS channel on RHEL 8.
#   - Installs rsyslog and the rsyslog-gnutls TLS driver
#   - Enforces:  $DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls
#                $ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1
#                $ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name
#   - Comments out any conflicting (cleartext) settings
#   - Persists across reboots (config files + enabled service)
#   - Idempotent: safe to run repeatedly
#
set -euo pipefail

if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

MAIN_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
DROPIN_CONF="${DROPIN_DIR}/50-remote-tls.conf"
CHANGED=0

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Ensure rsyslog and the GnuTLS network stream driver are installed
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
for pkg in rsyslog rsyslog-gnutls; do
    if ! rpm -q "$pkg" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf -y install "$pkg"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done

mkdir -p "$DROPIN_DIR"
[[ -f "$MAIN_CONF" ]] || { touch "$MAIN_CONF"; CHANGED=1; }

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Comment out conflicting/cleartext directives everywhere they may exist
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
comment_conflicts() {
    local file="$1"
    [[ -f "$file" ]] || return 0

    # Wrong netstream driver (anything other than gtls)
    if grep -Eiq '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+(?!gtls)' "$file" 2>/dev/null || \
       grep -Ei '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+\S+' "$file" | grep -Eviq 'gtls\s*$'; then
        sed -ri 's/^(\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+)(?!gtls)/#\1/I' "$file" 2>/dev/null || true
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            :
        done < /dev/null
        # Portable approach without PCRE lookahead:
        sed -ri '/^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+/I { /gtls\s*$/I!s/^/#/ }' "$file"
        CHANGED=1
    fi

    # StreamDriverMode set to anything other than 1
    if grep -Ei '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode\s+\S+' "$file" | grep -Eviq '\s1\s*$'; then
        sed -ri '/^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode\s+/I { /\s1\s*$/I!s/^/#/ }' "$file"
        CHANGED=1
    fi

    # AuthMode set to something other than x509/name
    if grep -Ei '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+\S+' "$file" | grep -Eviq 'x509\/name\s*$'; then
        sed -ri '/^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+/I { /x509\/name\s*$/I!s/^/#/ }' "$file"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
}

comment_conflicts "$MAIN_CONF"
for f in "$DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf; do
    [[ -e "$f" ]] || continue
    [[ "$f" == "$DROPIN_CONF" ]] && continue
    comment_conflicts "$f"
done

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Ensure the required TLS directives are present (managed drop-in file)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ensure_directive() {
    # ensure_directive <directive> <value>
    local directive="$1" value="$2"
    # Present and correct anywhere (main conf or drop-ins)?
    if grep -Eirq "^\s*\\${directive}\s+${value//\//\\/}\s*$" "$MAIN_CONF" "$DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    # Add/fix in the managed drop-in file
    touch "$DROPIN_CONF"
    if grep -Eiq "^\s*\\${directive}\s+" "$DROPIN_CONF"; then
        sed -ri "s|^\s*\\${directive}\s+.*|${directive} ${value}|I" "$DROPIN_CONF"
    else
        printf '%s %s\n' "$directive" "$value" >> "$DROPIN_CONF"
    fi
    CHANGED=1
}

if [[ ! -f "$DROPIN_CONF" ]]; then
    cat > "$DROPIN_CONF" <<'EOF'
# Managed by security remediation: encrypted (TLS) off-loading of log records
EOF
    CHANGED=1
fi

ensure_directive '$Def

[[ -f "$MAIN_CONF" ]] || { touch "$MAIN_CONF"; CHANGED=1; }

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Comment out conflicting/cleartext directives everywhere they may exist
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
comment_conflicts() {
    local file="$1"
    [[ -f "$file" ]] || return 0

    # Wrong netstream driver (anything other than gtls)
    if grep -Eiq '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+(?!gtls)' "$file" 2>/dev/null || \
       grep -Ei '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+\S+' "$file" | grep -Eviq 'gtls\s*$'; then
        sed -ri 's/^(\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+)(?!gtls)/#\1/I' "$file" 2>/dev/null || true
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            :
        done < /dev/null
        # Portable approach without PCRE lookahead:
        sed -ri '/^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+/I { /gtls\s*$/I!s/^/#/ }' "$file"
        CHANGED=1
    fi

    # StreamDriverMode set to anything other than 1
    if grep -Ei '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode\s+\S+' "$file" | grep -Eviq '\s1\s*$'; then
        sed -ri '/^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode\s+/I { /\s1\s*$/I!s/^/#/ }' "$file"
        CHANGED=1
    fi

    # AuthMode set to something other than x509/name
    if grep -Ei '^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+\S+' "$file" | grep -Eviq 'x509\/name\s*$'; then
        sed -ri '/^\s*\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode\s+/I { /x509\/name\s*$/I!s/^/#/ }' "$file"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
}

comment_conflicts "$MAIN_CONF"
for f in "$DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf; do
    [[ -e "$f" ]] || continue
    [[ "$f" == "$DROPIN_CONF" ]] && continue
    comment_conflicts "$f"
done

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Ensure the required TLS directives are present (managed drop-in file)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ensure_directive() {
    # ensure_directive <directive> <value>
    local directive="$1" value="$2"
    # Present and correct anywhere (main conf or drop-ins)?
    if grep -Eirq "^\s*\\${directive}\s+${value//\//\\/}\s*$" "$MAIN_CONF" "$DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    # Add/fix in the managed drop-in file
    touch "$DROPIN_CONF"
    if grep -Eiq "^\s*\\${directive}\s+" "$DROPIN_CONF"; then
        sed -ri "s|^\s*\\${directive}\s+.*|${directive} ${value}|I" "$DROPIN_CONF"
    else
        printf '%s %s\n' "$directive" "$value" >> "$DROPIN_CONF"
    fi
    CHANGED=1
}

if [[ ! -f "$DROPIN_CONF" ]]; then
    cat > "$DROPIN_CONF" <<'EOF'
# Managed by security remediation: encrypted (TLS) off-loading of log records
EOF
    CHANGED=1
fi

ensure_directive '$Def

EOF

    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

changed=0

    chown root:root "$BANNER_FILE"
